API Reference

This section provides auto-generated API documentation for all public OpenViper modules. Every class, function, and exception is documented with its signature, parameters, and description.

On this page

  • openviper (top-level)

  • openviper.app

  • openviper.http

  • openviper.routing

  • openviper.db

  • openviper.auth

  • openviper.serializers

  • openviper.middleware

  • openviper.tasks

  • openviper.ai

  • openviper.conf

  • openviper.core

  • openviper.utils

  • openviper.staticfiles

  • openviper.storage

openviper (top-level)

OpenViper - A production-ready, high-performance Python web framework, with modern async capabilities.

class openviper.OpenViper(debug=None, middleware=None, title=None, version=None, openapi_url=None, docs_url=None, redoc_url=None)[source]

Bases: object

The OpenViper ASGI application.

This class acts as both an ASGI callable and a router decorator, so you can register routes directly on the app instance.

Parameters:

  • debug (bool | None) – Enable debug mode (overrides settings.DEBUG when set).

  • middleware (list[Any] | None) – Extra middleware to prepend to the stack.

  • title (str | None) – OpenAPI document title.

  • version (str | None) – API version string.

  • openapi_url (str | None) – URL to serve the OpenAPI JSON schema.

  • docs_url (str | None) – URL for Swagger UI.

  • redoc_url (str | None) – URL for ReDoc UI.

Example

>>> app = OpenViper(title="My API", version="1.0.0")
>>> @app.get("/ping")
... async def ping(request):
...     return {"pong": True}
debug
title
version
openapi_url
docs_url
redoc_url
router

Mount a sub-router.

Parameters:

  • router (Router) – The Router to mount.

  • prefix (str) – Optional prefix to prepend to all routes.

Return type:

None

Register a startup handler.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Callable[[…], Any]

Register a shutdown handler.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

exception_handler(exc_class)[source]

Decorator to register a custom exception handler.

Example

>>> @app.exception_handler(ValueError)
... async def handle_value_error(request, exc):
...     return JSONResponse({"error": str(exc)}, status_code=400)
Parameters:

exc_class (type[Exception])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

invalidate_openapi_schema()[source]

Force schema regeneration on next request.

Return type:

None

run(host='127.0.0.1', port=8000, reload=True, log_level='info', workers=1)[source]

Start a uvicorn development server.

Prefer using python viperctl.py runserver for project use.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Bind address.

  • port (int) – Bind port.

  • reload (bool) – Enable auto-reload on code changes.

  • log_level (str) – Uvicorn log level string.

  • workers (int) – Number of worker processes (reload must be False).

Return type:

None

test_client(**kwargs)[source]

Return an httpx.AsyncClient configured for this app.

Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

Return type:

AsyncClient

description
class openviper.Request(scope, receive=None)[source]

Bases: object

Encapsulates an HTTP request.

This class wraps the raw ASGI scope and receive callable, providing convenient access to all request components: method, URL, headers, query parameters, path parameters, cookies, body, and JSON payload.

Parameters:

  • scope (dict[str, Any]) – ASGI connection scope dict.

  • receive (Any) – ASGI receive callable.

path_params: dict[str, Any]
state: dict[str, Any]
user: Any
auth: Any
property method: str
property url: URL
property path: str
property root_path: str
property headers: Headers
property query_params: QueryParams
property cookies: dict[str, str]
property client: tuple[str, int] | None
header(name)[source]

O(1) raw header lookup.

Builds a bytes → bytes map from the ASGI scope headers on the first call and caches it for the lifetime of the request. Header names are guaranteed to be lower-cased by the ASGI server.

Parameters:

name (bytes) – Lower-cased header name (e.g. b"content-type").

Returns:

The raw header value, or None if the header is absent.

Return type:

bytes | None

async body()[source]

Read the full raw request body.

Return type:

bytes

async json()[source]

Parse the request body as JSON.

Return type:

Any

async form()[source]

Parse application/x-www-form-urlencoded or multipart form data.

Return type:

ImmutableMultiDict

async stream()[source]

Iterate over the raw body in chunks.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[bytes]

is_secure()[source]
Return type:

bool

class openviper.Response(content=None, status_code=200, headers=None, media_type=None)[source]

Bases: object

Base HTTP response.

Parameters:

  • content (Any) – Response body. Bytes, str, or None.

  • status_code (int) – HTTP status code (default 200).

  • headers (dict[str, str] | None) – Optional dict of headers to set.

  • media_type (str | None) – Content-Type MIME type.

charset: str = 'utf-8'
status_code
media_type
body
property headers: MutableHeaders
set_cookie(key, value='', max_age=None, expires=None, path='/', domain=None, secure=False, httponly=False, samesite='lax')[source]

  • value (str)

  • expires (int | None)

  • path (str)

  • domain (str | None)

Return type:

None

delete_cookie(key, path='/', domain=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • domain (str | None)

Return type:

None

class openviper.JSONResponse(content=None, status_code=200, headers=None, indent=None)[source]

Bases: Response

JSON response serialised with orjson.

Parameters:

  • content (Any)

class openviper.Router(prefix='', middlewares=None)[source]

Bases: object

URL router that collects routes and resolves requests.

Supports route groups (sub-routers) via include().

Example

>>> router = Router(prefix="/api")
>>> @router.get("/users")
... async def list_users(request): ...
Parameters:

  • prefix (str)

  • middlewares (list[Middleware] | None)

route(path, methods, name=None, middlewares=None)[source]

Decorator to register a handler for a path and methods.

Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • methods (list[str])

  • name (str | None)

  • middlewares (list[Callable[[Any, Any], Awaitable[Any]]] | None)

Return type:

Callable[[Callable[[…], Awaitable[Any]]], Callable[[…], Awaitable[Any]]]

add(path, handler, methods=None, namespace=None, middlewares=None)[source]

Add a route directly without using decorators.

Parameters:

  • path (str) – URL path pattern

  • handler (Callable[[...], Awaitable[Any]]) – Handler function

  • methods (list[str] | None) – HTTP methods (default: [“GET”])

  • namespace (str | None) – Route name (default: handler.__name__)

  • middlewares (list[Callable[[Any, Any], Awaitable[Any]]] | None) – Route-specific middlewares

Return type:

None

include_router(router)[source]

Mount a sub-router, prefixing all its routes with this router’s prefix.

Parameters:

router (Router)

Return type:

None

property routes: list[Route]

All routes including sub-router routes, flattened (cached).

resolve(method, path)[source]

Find the matching route for a path and method.

Returns:

(route, path_params) tuple.

Raises:

  • NotFound – No route matched the path.

  • MethodNotAllowed – Path matched but method not allowed.

Parameters:

  • method (str)

  • path (str)

Return type:

tuple[Route, dict[str, Any]]

url_for(name, **path_params)[source]

Reverse-resolve a URL by route name.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Route name as registered.

  • **path_params (Any) – Values to fill in path parameters.

Returns:

URL string.

Raises:

KeyError – Route name not found.

Return type:

str

openviper.include(router, prefix='')[source]

Helper to include a sub-router with an optional additional prefix.

Creates a new router whose route paths are all prefixed with the given prefix. The original router is left unchanged.

Example

>>> from myapp.routes import router as user_router
>>> app.include_router(include(user_router, prefix="/api/v1"))
Parameters:

  • router (Router)

  • prefix (str)

Return type:

Router

exception openviper.OpenViperException[source]

Bases: Exception

Base exception for all OpenViper errors.

Bases: OpenViperException

Raised to return an HTTP error response.

Parameters:

  • status_code (int) – HTTP status code.

  • detail (Any) – Human-readable error detail.

  • headers (dict[str, str] | None) – Optional extra headers to include in the response.

Return type:

None

status_code
detail
headers

404 Not Found.

openviper.setup(force=False)[source]

Initialize Openviper settings and registry explicitly.

This function should be called before importing any other Openviper components that rely on settings.

Parameters:

force (bool)

Return type:

None

openviper.app

OpenViper ASGI application.

The central OpenViper class is the entry point for all request handling. It ties together routing, middleware, dependency injection, exception handling, and OpenAPI schema generation.

Example

from openviper import OpenViper

app = OpenViper()

@app.get("/")
async def hello(request):
    return {"message": "Hello, World!"}
class openviper.app.OpenViper(debug=None, middleware=None, title=None, version=None, openapi_url=None, docs_url=None, redoc_url=None)[source]

Bases: object

The OpenViper ASGI application.

This class acts as both an ASGI callable and a router decorator, so you can register routes directly on the app instance.

Parameters:

  • debug (bool | None) – Enable debug mode (overrides settings.DEBUG when set).

  • middleware (list[Any] | None) – Extra middleware to prepend to the stack.

  • title (str | None) – OpenAPI document title.

  • version (str | None) – API version string.

  • openapi_url (str | None) – URL to serve the OpenAPI JSON schema.

  • docs_url (str | None) – URL for Swagger UI.

  • redoc_url (str | None) – URL for ReDoc UI.

Example

>>> app = OpenViper(title="My API", version="1.0.0")
>>> @app.get("/ping")
... async def ping(request):
...     return {"pong": True}
debug
title
version
openapi_url
docs_url
redoc_url
router

on_startup(func)[source]

Register a startup handler.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

on_shutdown(func)[source]

Register a shutdown handler.

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

exception_handler(exc_class)[source]

Decorator to register a custom exception handler.

Example

>>> @app.exception_handler(ValueError)
... async def handle_value_error(request, exc):
...     return JSONResponse({"error": str(exc)}, status_code=400)
Parameters:

exc_class (type[Exception])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

invalidate_openapi_schema()[source]

Force schema regeneration on next request.

Return type:

None

run(host='127.0.0.1', port=8000, reload=True, log_level='info', workers=1)[source]

Start a uvicorn development server.

Prefer using python viperctl.py runserver for project use.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Bind address.

  • port (int) – Bind port.

  • reload (bool) – Enable auto-reload on code changes.

  • log_level (str) – Uvicorn log level string.

  • workers (int) – Number of worker processes (reload must be False).

Return type:

None

kwargs (Any)

AsyncClient

class openviper.http.request.Request(scope, receive=None)[source]

Bases: object

Encapsulates an HTTP request.

This class wraps the raw ASGI scope and receive callable, providing convenient access to all request components: method, URL, headers, query parameters, path parameters, cookies, body, and JSON payload.

Parameters:

  • scope (dict[str, Any]) – ASGI connection scope dict.

  • receive (Any) – ASGI receive callable.

path_params: dict[str, Any]
state: dict[str, Any]
user: Any
auth: Any
property method: str
property url: URL
property path: str
property root_path: str
property headers: Headers
property query_params: QueryParams
property cookies: dict[str, str]
property client: tuple[str, int] | None
header(name)[source]

O(1) raw header lookup.

Builds a bytes → bytes map from the ASGI scope headers on the first call and caches it for the lifetime of the request. Header names are guaranteed to be lower-cased by the ASGI server.

Parameters:

name (bytes) – Lower-cased header name (e.g. b"content-type").

Returns:

The raw header value, or None if the header is absent.

Return type:

bytes | None

async body()[source]

Read the full raw request body.

Return type:

bytes

async json()[source]

Parse the request body as JSON.

Return type:

Any

async form()[source]

Parse application/x-www-form-urlencoded or multipart form data.

Return type:

ImmutableMultiDict

async stream()[source]

Iterate over the raw body in chunks.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[bytes]

is_secure()[source]
Return type:

bool

class openviper.http.response.GZipResponse(content, minimum_size=500, compresslevel=9)[source]

Bases: Response

Response with gzip-compressed body.

Parameters:

  • content (Response)

  • minimum_size (int)

  • compresslevel (int)

Views

Class-based views for OpenViper.

Provides View — a base class that dispatches incoming requests to handler methods named after the HTTP verb (get, post, put, patch, delete, head, options).

Usage with a router:

from openviper.http.views import View
from openviper.http.response import JSONResponse

class UserListView(View):
    async def get(self, request):
        users = await User.objects.all()
        return JSONResponse([u._to_dict() for u in users])

    async def post(self, request):
        data = await request.json()
        user = await User.objects.create(**data)
        return JSONResponse(user._to_dict(), status_code=201)

router.route("/users", methods=["GET", "POST"])(UserListView.as_view())
# — or use the shorthand —
UserListView.register(router, "/users")
class openviper.http.views.View(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: object

Base class-based view.

Subclass and implement one or more HTTP-verb methods (get, post, etc.). Unimplemented methods return 405 Method Not Allowed.

Class attributes:
http_method_names: Iterable of lowercase method names this view

is willing to handle. Defaults to all standard methods but can be overridden per-subclass.

Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

http_method_names: list[str] = ['get', 'post', 'put', 'patch', 'delete', 'head', 'options']
serializer_class: Any = None

Optional Pydantic serializer class for request body schema. When set, the OpenAPI schema generator uses it to produce the requestBody entry so that Swagger UI displays input fields.

async dispatch(request, **kwargs)[source]

Route request to the appropriate handler method.

If the HTTP method is not in http_method_names or if the subclass does not define a handler for it, return a 405 response.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

Response

http_method_not_allowed(request, **kwargs)[source]

Return a 405 Method Not Allowed error.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

Any

async options(request, **kwargs)[source]

Handle OPTIONS by returning allowed methods in the Allow header.

Parameters:

  • request (Request)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

Response

classmethod as_view(**initkwargs)[source]

Return an async callable suitable for use as a route handler.

Any initkwargs are forwarded to the view’s __init__ and stored on every instance created per request.

Example:

router.get("/items")(ItemView.as_view())
Parameters:

initkwargs (Any)

Return type:

Any

classmethod register(router, path, *, name=None, **initkwargs)[source]

Register this view on router at path.

Automatically determines which HTTP methods are implemented and registers them.

Example:

UserView.register(router, "/users")
Parameters:

  • router (Router)

  • path (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • initkwargs (Any)

Return type:

None

openviper.routing

URL routing engine for OpenViper.

Supports:

  • Literal path segments: /users

  • String parameters: /users/{id}

  • Typed parameters: /users/{id:int}, /files/{path:path}

  • HTTP method routing

  • Sub-routers (blueprints) with include()

  • Middleware attachment per-router

class openviper.routing.router.Route(path, methods, handler, name=None, middlewares=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

A single registered route.

Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • methods (set[str])

  • handler (Callable[[...], Awaitable[Any]])

  • name (str | None)

  • middlewares (list[Callable[[Any, Any], Awaitable[Any]]])

path: str

URL path template.

methods: set[str]

Allowed HTTP methods.

handler: Callable[[...], Awaitable[Any]]

Async callable that handles the request.

name: str | None = None

Optional name for reverse URL generation.

middlewares: list[Callable[[Any, Any], Awaitable[Any]]]

Per-route middleware stack.

match(path)[source]

Return path params dict if path matches, else None.

Parameters:

path (str)

Return type:

dict[str, Any] | None

openviper.routing.router.include(router, prefix='')[source]

Helper to include a sub-router with an optional additional prefix.

Creates a new router whose route paths are all prefixed with the given prefix. The original router is left unchanged.

Example

>>> from myapp.routes import router as user_router
>>> app.include_router(include(user_router, prefix="/api/v1"))
Parameters:

  • router (Router)

  • prefix (str)

Return type:

Router

openviper.db

Models

OpenViper ORM - Model base classes, fields, and query API.

Built on top of SQLAlchemy Core for cross-database support with an ergonomic high-level API.

Example

from openviper.db.models import Model
from openviper.db import fields

class Post(Model):
    class Meta:
        table_name = "posts"

    title = fields.CharField(max_length=255)
    body  = fields.TextField()
    created_at = fields.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

# Query
posts = await Post.objects.filter(title__contains="OpenViper").all()
class openviper.db.models.ModelMeta(name, bases, namespace)[source]

Bases: type

Metaclass that collects field definitions and wires up the Manager.

Automatically generates table_name as {app_name}_{model_name} in snake_case if not explicitly specified in Meta.table_name.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • bases (tuple[type, ...])

  • namespace (dict[str, Any])

Return type:

ModelMeta

registry: ClassVar[dict[str, type[Model]]] = {'auth.ContentType': <class 'openviper.auth.models.ContentType'>, 'auth.ContentTypePermission': <class 'openviper.auth.models.ContentTypePermission'>, 'auth.Permission': <class 'openviper.auth.models.Permission'>, 'auth.Role': <class 'openviper.auth.models.Role'>, 'auth.RolePermission': <class 'openviper.auth.models.RolePermission'>, 'auth.RoleProfile': <class 'openviper.auth.models.RoleProfile'>, 'auth.RoleProfileDetail': <class 'openviper.auth.models.RoleProfileDetail'>, 'auth.User': <class 'openviper.auth.models.User'>, 'auth.UserRole': <class 'openviper.auth.models.UserRole'>}

class openviper.db.models.QuerySet(model, ignore_permissions=False)[source]

Bases: object

Lazy chainable query builder.

Filters, ordering and slicing are accumulated until awaited.

Example

>>> qs = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by("-created_at")
>>> users = await qs.all()
>>> count = await qs.count()
Parameters:

  • model (type[Model])

  • ignore_permissions (bool)

kwargs (Any)

Return type:

QuerySet

class openviper.db.models.Model(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: object

Base model class. Inherit to define database-backed models.

Lifecycle Hooks

Override any of these async methods in your model to hook into the persistence lifecycle. All default implementations are no-ops.

Create flow (pk is None):

before_validate → validate → before_insert → before_save
→ INSERT → after_insert → on_change

Update flow (pk is set):

before_validate → validate → before_save
→ UPDATE → on_update → on_change  (only if data changed)

Delete flow:

on_delete → DELETE → after_delete

Example

class Article(Model):
    title = CharField(max_length=200)
    body  = TextField()
    author_id = IntegerField()
    published_at = DateTimeField(null=True)

    async def before_save(self) -> None:
        self.title = self.title.strip()

    async def validate(self) -> None:
        if not self.title:
            raise ValueError("Title is required")

    async def after_insert(self) -> None:
        print(f"Article {self.pk} created!")

    async def on_change(self, previous_state: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        print(f"Changed fields: {previous_state}")
objects: ClassVar[Manager]

The default Manager for query building.

id = IntegerField(name='id')
property content_type: str

Return the content type identifier for this model (instance or class).

Format: ‘app_label.model_name’ (e.g., ‘auth.User’).

classmethod get_content_type_label()[source]

Return the content type identifier for this model class.

Return type:

str

property has_changed: bool

True if any field value differs from the last-saved state.

async before_validate()[source]

Called before validate() during both create and update.

Return type:

None

async validate()[source]

Validate all field values using each field’s validate() method.

Collects every field-level error and raises a single ValueError listing all failures. Override in subclasses to add custom business-rule validation (call await super().validate() first to keep the built-in checks).

Fields that have been soft-removed (via migrations) are automatically excluded from validation.

Return type:

None

async before_insert()[source]

Called only on create, after validation but before the INSERT.

Return type:

None

async before_save()[source]

Called on both create and update, immediately before the DB write.

Return type:

None

async after_insert()[source]

Called only on create, after the INSERT has succeeded.

Return type:

None

async on_update()[source]

Called only on update, after the UPDATE has succeeded.

Return type:

None

async on_delete()[source]

Called before the DELETE is issued. Raise to abort deletion.

Return type:

None

async after_delete()[source]

Called after a successful DELETE.

Return type:

None

async on_change(previous_state)[source]

Called after create or update if field values actually changed.

Parameters:

previous_state (dict[str, Any]) – Dict of {field_name: old_value} for every field that differed from the saved state.

Return type:

None

property pk: Any

Primary key (aliased to id by default).

async save(ignore_permissions=False)[source]

Persist this instance to the database (INSERT or UPDATE).

Executes the full lifecycle hook chain:

  • Create (pk is None): before_validate → validate → before_insert → before_save → INSERT → after_insert → on_change

  • Update (pk set): before_validate → validate → before_save → UPDATE → on_update → on_change (only when data actually changed)

Parameters:

ignore_permissions (bool)

Return type:

None

async delete(ignore_permissions=False)[source]

Delete this instance from the database.

Executes: on_delete → DELETE → after_delete. If on_delete() raises, the DELETE is aborted.

Parameters:

ignore_permissions (bool)

Return type:

None

async refresh_from_db()[source]

Reload all fields from the database.

Return type:

None

Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

class openviper.db.models.AbstractModel(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Abstract base model — subclasses share fields but no table is created.

Use for common timestamp mixins:

class TimestampMixin(AbstractModel):
    created_at = DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = DateTimeField(auto_now=True)

class Post(TimestampMixin):
    title = CharField(max_length=255)
Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

abstract = True
objects: ClassVar[Manager]

The default Manager for query building.

Fields

ORM field definitions for OpenViper models.

class openviper.db.fields.Field(primary_key=False, null=False, blank=False, unique=False, db_index=False, default=None, db_column=None, auto_increment=False, choices=None, help_text='')[source]

Bases: object

Base field descriptor. :param primary_key: Mark this field as the table primary key. :param null: Allow NULL values. :param blank: Allow empty strings (validation-only flag). :param unique: Add a UNIQUE constraint. :param db_index: Add a database index. :param default: Default value or callable. :param db_column: Override the column name in the database. :param auto_increment: Auto-increment the column (integer PK). :param choices: List of (db_value, display_value) allowed values. :param help_text: Human-readable description.

Parameters:

  • primary_key (bool)

  • null (bool)

  • blank (bool)

  • unique (bool)

  • db_index (bool)

  • default (Any)

  • db_column (str | None)

  • auto_increment (bool)

  • choices (list[tuple[Any, str]] | None)

  • help_text (str)

property column_name: str

Parameters:

value (Any)

Return type:

Any

to_db(value)[source]
Parameters:

value (Any)

Return type:

Any

validate(value)[source]
Parameters:

value (Any)

Return type:

None

class openviper.db.fields.FileField(upload_to='uploads/', max_file_size=None, max_length=512, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: CharField

File upload field.

Stores the relative file path in the database as a VARCHAR column. The actual file is persisted through the storage backend.

Parameters:

  • upload_to (str) – Sub-directory under MEDIA_ROOT for uploaded files.

  • max_file_size (int | None) – Maximum allowed file size in bytes. None means use the global MAX_FILE_SIZE setting.

  • max_length (int) – Maximum path string length in the database.

  • kwargs (Any)

property max_file_size: int

Return the effective max file size in bytes.

validate(value)[source]

Validate the value.

If value is a raw bytes/file-like object, check size constraints. If value is a string path (already saved), run normal CharField validation.

Parameters:

value (Any)

Return type:

None

class openviper.db.fields.ImageField(upload_to='images/', max_file_size=None, allowed_extensions=None, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: FileField

Image upload field.

Behaves like FileField but restricts uploads to image content types.

Parameters:

  • upload_to (str) – Sub-directory under MEDIA_ROOT for uploaded images.

  • max_file_size (int | None) – Maximum allowed file size in bytes.

  • allowed_extensions (set[str] | frozenset[str] | None) – Set of permitted file extensions (lowercase, without dot).

  • kwargs (Any)

DEFAULT_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'bmp', 'gif', 'ico', 'jpeg', 'jpg', 'png', 'svg', 'tiff', 'webp'})
validate(value)[source]

Validate the value.

If value is a raw bytes/file-like object, check size constraints. If value is a string path (already saved), run normal CharField validation.

Parameters:

value (Any)

Return type:

None

Connection

Database connection management using SQLAlchemy async engine.

openviper.db.connection.get_metadata()[source]
Return type:

MetaData

async openviper.db.connection.get_engine()[source]

Return (or lazily create) the shared async engine.

Uses double-checked locking: the fast path reads _engine without acquiring the lock. The lock is acquired only when _engine is None, preventing two coroutines from both entering _create_engine.

Return type:

AsyncEngine

async openviper.db.connection.get_connection()[source]

Return an async database connection from the pool.

Return type:

AsyncConnection

async openviper.db.connection.init_db(drop_first=False)[source]

Create all registered tables. Optionally drop them first.

Parameters:

drop_first (bool) – If True, drop all tables before creating them.

Return type:

None

async openviper.db.connection.close_db()[source]

Dispose the engine and all pooled connections.

Return type:

None

async openviper.db.connection.configure_db(database_url, echo=False)[source]

Explicitly configure the database engine (call before init_db).

Disposes any existing engine before replacing it so that pooled connections are not leaked.

Parameters:

  • database_url (str)

  • echo (bool)

Return type:

None

Executor

Low-level SQL execution helpers used by the ORM layer.

openviper.db.executor.invalidate_soft_removed_cache()[source]

Clear the soft-removed column cache (call after migrations).

Return type:

None

openviper.db.executor.get_soft_removed_columns(table_name)[source]

Return the frozenset of soft-removed column names for a table (sync).

Must call _load_soft_removed_columns() first in an async context. Values are frozenset so callers can iterate without holding any lock.

Parameters:

table_name (str)

Return type:

frozenset[str]

openviper.db.executor.get_table(model_cls)[source]

Return (or lazily build) the SQLAlchemy Table for a model class.

Parameters:

model_cls (type[Model])

Return type:

sa.Table

async openviper.db.executor.execute_select(qs)[source]
Parameters:

qs (QuerySet)

Return type:

list[dict[str, Any]]

async openviper.db.executor.execute_count(qs)[source]
Parameters:

qs (QuerySet)

Return type:

int

async openviper.db.executor.execute_delete(qs)[source]
Parameters:

qs (QuerySet)

Return type:

int

async openviper.db.executor.execute_update(qs, values)[source]
Parameters:

  • qs (QuerySet)

  • values (dict[str, Any])

Return type:

int

async openviper.db.executor.execute_save(instance, ignore_permissions=False)[source]

INSERT or UPDATE a single model instance.

Parameters:

  • instance (Model)

None

class openviper.auth.models.AbstractUser(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Built-in user model.

Passwords are stored hashed via Argon2id (with bcrypt fallback).

Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

username = CharField(name='username')

Unique login identifier.

email = EmailField(name='email')

Unique email address.

password = CharField(name='password')

Hashed password — never set this directly; use set_password().

first_name = CharField(name='first_name')
last_name = CharField(name='last_name')
is_active = BooleanField(name='is_active')

Soft-delete flag.

is_superuser = BooleanField(name='is_superuser')

Grant all permissions when True.

is_staff = BooleanField(name='is_staff')

Allow access to the admin interface.

role_profile = ForeignKey(name='role_profile')
created_at = DateTimeField(name='created_at')

Account creation timestamp.

updated_at = DateTimeField(name='updated_at')

Last modification timestamp.

last_login = DateTimeField(name='last_login')

Last successful login timestamp.

Bases: object

abstract = True

Backends

Authentication backends for OpenViper.

Any | None

Decorators

Auth decorators for view-level access control.

Example

from openviper.auth.decorators import login_required, permission_required, role_required

@app.get("/dashboard")
@login_required
async def dashboard(request):
    return {"user": request.user.username}

@app.delete("/admin/users/{id}")
@permission_required("user.delete")
async def delete_user(request, id: int):
    ...

@app.get("/reports")
@role_required("manager")
async def reports(request):
    ...
openviper.auth.decorators.login_required(func)[source]

Require the request user to be authenticated.

Raises:

Unauthorized (401) – User is not authenticated.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

openviper.auth.decorators.permission_required(codename)[source]

Require the request user to have a specific permission.

Parameters:

codename (str) – Permission codename, e.g. "post.create".

Raises:

  • Unauthorized (401) – User is not authenticated.

  • PermissionDenied (403) – User lacks the required permission.

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

openviper.auth.decorators.role_required(role_name)[source]

Require the request user to have a specific role.

Parameters:

role_name (str) – Role name, e.g. "admin".

Raises:

  • Unauthorized (401) – User is not authenticated.

  • PermissionDenied (403) – User lacks the required role.

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

openviper.auth.decorators.superuser_required(func)[source]

Require request.user.is_superuser to be True.

Raises:

  • Unauthorized (401) – User is not authenticated.

  • PermissionDenied (403) – User is not a superuser.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

openviper.auth.decorators.staff_required(func)[source]

Require request.user.is_staff to be True.

Parameters:

func (Callable[[...], Any])

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

JWT

JWT token creation and verification for OpenViper.

Uses python-jose under the hood with HS256 algorithm (RS256 configurable).

openviper.auth.jwt.create_access_token(user_id, extra_claims=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • user_id (int | str)

  • extra_claims (dict[str, Any] | None)

Return type:

str

openviper.auth.jwt.create_refresh_token(user_id)[source]
Parameters:

user_id (int | str)

Return type:

str

openviper.auth.jwt.decode_token_unverified(token)[source]

Return the claims from a JWT without verifying signature or expiry.

Used only by the logout path to extract jti/exp so the token can be added to the blocklist even if it has already expired.

Parameters:

token (str) – Encoded JWT string.

Returns:

Unverified claims dict, or an empty dict if the token is malformed.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

openviper.auth.jwt.decode_access_token(token)[source]

Decode and verify a JWT access token.

Parameters:

token (str) – Encoded JWT string.

Returns:

Decoded claims dict.

Raises:

  • TokenExpired – Token has passed its expiry.

  • AuthenticationFailed – Token is invalid or malformed.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

openviper.auth.jwt.decode_refresh_token(token)[source]

Decode and verify a JWT refresh token.

Parameters:

token (str) – Encoded JWT string.

Returns:

Decoded claims dict.

Raises:

  • TokenExpired – Token has passed its expiry.

  • AuthenticationFailed – Token is invalid or malformed.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Sessions

Session management for OpenViper.

Sessions are stored in the database (openviper_sessions table). The session ID is a cryptographically random token stored in a cookie.

openviper.auth.sessions.generate_session_key()[source]
Return type:

str

async openviper.auth.sessions.create_session(user_id, data=None)[source]

Create a new session for the given user.

Parameters:

  • user_id (int) – The authenticated user’s primary key.

  • data (dict[str, Any] | None) – Optional extra data to store in the session.

Returns:

The session key (store in a cookie).

Return type:

str

async openviper.auth.sessions.get_user_from_session(cookie_header)[source]

Parse the cookie header and look up the user from the session store.

Parameters:

cookie_header (str) – Raw Cookie header value.

Returns:

User instance or None.

Return type:

Any | None

async openviper.auth.sessions.delete_session(session_key)[source]

Invalidate a session (logout) and prune expired sessions.

Parameters:

session_key (str)

Return type:

None

Hashers

Password hashing using Argon2id (primary) or bcrypt (fallback).

openviper.auth.hashers.make_password(raw_password, algorithm='argon2')[source]

Hash a raw password.

Parameters:

  • raw_password (str) – The plaintext password.

  • algorithm (str) – “argon2” (default), “bcrypt”, or “plain” (testing only).

Returns:

The hashed password string (prefixed with algorithm identifier).

Return type:

str

openviper.auth.hashers.check_password(raw_password, hashed_password)[source]

Verify a raw password against a stored hash.

Parameters:

  • raw_password (str) – User-supplied password.

  • hashed_password (str) – Stored hash (prefixed with algorithm).

Returns:

True if the password matches, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

openviper.auth.hashers.is_password_usable(hashed_password)[source]

Return True if the hash is usable (not a sentinel disabled value).

Parameters:

hashed_password (str | None)

Return type:

bool

openviper.auth.hashers.make_unusable_password()[source]

Return a sentinel string that will never match any password.

Return type:

str

Permissions

Role-based permission enforcement logic.

exception openviper.auth.permissions.PermissionError[source]

Bases: Exception

Raised when a user attempts an unauthorized action on a model.

async openviper.auth.permissions.check_permission_for_model(model_cls, action, ignore_permissions=False)[source]

Check if the current request user is authorized to perform an action.

Parameters:

  • model_cls (type[Model]) – The model class being accessed.

  • action (str) – ‘create’, ‘read’, ‘write’, ‘update’, or ‘delete’.

  • ignore_permissions (bool) – If True, bypass all permission checks.

Raises:

PermissionError – If the user is unauthenticated or unauthorized.

Return type:

None

openviper.serializers

Pydantic-based serializer/schema base for OpenViper.

Provides Serializer as a thin convenience layer over pydantic.BaseModel

full Pydantic v2 power available.

ModelSerializer goes a step further: it introspects a OpenViper Model class and auto-generates Pydantic fields so you never have to duplicate your schema definition.

Usage:

from openviper.serializers import Serializer, ModelSerializer
from myapp.models import Post

# Manual serializer
class PostSerializer(Serializer):
    title: str
    body: str
    tags: list[str] = []

# Auto-generated from model
class PostSerializer(ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = Post
        fields = "__all__"

# Validate incoming data
post = PostSerializer.validate({"title": "Hello", "body": "World"})

# Serialize an ORM model instance
data = PostSerializer.from_orm(db_post).serialize()

Field Validation:

from openviper.serializers import Serializer, field_validator

class UserSerializer(Serializer):
    username: str
    age: int

    @field_validator("username")
    @classmethod
    def validate_username(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if len(v) < 3:
            raise ValueError("Username must be at least 3 characters")
        return v.lower()

    @field_validator("age")
    @classmethod
    def validate_age(cls, v: int) -> int:
        if v < 0 or v > 150:
            raise ValueError("Age must be between 0 and 150")
        return v

Model Validation (cross-field):

from openviper.serializers import Serializer, model_validator

class PasswordChangeSerializer(Serializer):
    password: str
    confirm_password: str

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def passwords_match(self) -> "PasswordChangeSerializer":
        if self.password != self.confirm_password:
            raise ValueError("Passwords do not match")
        return self
class openviper.serializers.base.Serializer[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Base class for all OpenViper serializers.

Extends pydantic.BaseModel with:

  • from_orm() — build from any ORM model using attribute access.

  • validate() — class-method parse that raises ValidationError (not the Pydantic variant) on failure.

  • serialize() — return a JSON-safe dict via model_dump().

  • serialize_many() — serialize a list of objects.

Field Validation

Use the @field_validator decorator to validate individual fields:

from openviper.serializers import Serializer, field_validator

class UserSerializer(Serializer):
    username: str
    email: str

    @field_validator("username")
    @classmethod
    def validate_username(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if len(v) < 3:
            raise ValueError("Username must be at least 3 characters")
        return v.lower()

    @field_validator("email")
    @classmethod
    def validate_email(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if "@" not in v:
            raise ValueError("Invalid email address")
        return v

Model Validation

Use the @model_validator decorator to validate across multiple fields:

from openviper.serializers import Serializer, model_validator

class PasswordSerializer(Serializer):
    password: str
    confirm_password: str

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def passwords_match(self) -> "PasswordSerializer":
        if self.password != self.confirm_password:
            raise ValueError("Passwords do not match")
        return self
model_config = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True, 'from_attributes': True, 'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_assignment': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

read_only_fields: ClassVar[tuple[str, ...]] = ()
write_only_fields: ClassVar[tuple[str, ...]] = ()
classmethod validate(data)[source]

Parse data into this serializer, raising ValidationError.

Parameters:

data (Any)

Return type:

T

classmethod validate_json_string(json_str)[source]

Parse a raw JSON string.

Parameters:

json_str (str)

Return type:

T

classmethod from_orm(obj)[source]

Construct from an ORM object (requires from_attributes=True).

Parameters:

obj (Any)

Return type:

T

classmethod from_orm_many(objs)[source]

Construct a list of serializer instances from a list of ORM objects.

Parameters:

objs (list[Any])

Return type:

list[T]

serialize(*, exclude=None)[source]

Return a JSON-safe dict, automatically excluding write-only fields.

Parameters:

exclude (set[str] | None)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

classmethod serialize_many(objs, *, exclude=None)[source]

Serialize a list of ORM objects to a list of dicts.

Parameters:

  • objs (list[Any])

  • exclude (set[str] | None)

Return type:

list[dict[str, Any]]

serialize_json(*, exclude=None)[source]

Return JSON bytes via pydantic-core’s Rust encoder.

Parameters:

exclude (set[str] | None)

Return type:

bytes

classmethod serialize_many_json(objs, *, exclude=None)[source]

Serialize a list of ORM objects to a JSON bytes array.

Parameters:

  • objs (list[Any])

  • exclude (set[str] | None)

Return type:

bytes

class openviper.serializers.base.ModelSerializer[source]

Bases: Serializer

Auto-generated serializer that derives fields from a OpenViper Model.

Subclass and define an inner Meta class:

class BlogSerializer(ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = Blog
        fields = "__all__"          # or ["title", "body"]
        # exclude = ["secret"]      # alternative to ``fields``
        # read_only_fields = ("id", "created_at")
        # extra_kwargs = {"title": {"required": False}}

ModelSerializer inherits every helper from Serializer (validate(), from_orm(), serialize(), etc.).

async create()[source]

Persist a new model instance from the validated data.

Returns the saved model instance.

Return type:

Any

async update(instance)[source]

Apply validated data to an existing model instance and save.

Returns the updated model instance.

Parameters:

instance (Any)

Return type:

Any

async save(instance=None)[source]

Create or update a model instance and return serialized data.

Behaviour: * If instance is provided explicitly → update that instance. * If the validated data contains a non-None id / pk

→ fetch the existing record and update it.

  • Otherwise → create a new record.

Returns:

A JSON-safe dict of the persisted model instance.

Parameters:

instance (Any | None)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

model_config = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True, 'from_attributes': True, 'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_assignment': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class openviper.serializers.base.PaginatedSerializer(*, count, next=None, previous=None, results)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Envelope for paginated list responses.

Parameters:

  • count (int)

  • next (str | None)

  • previous (str | None)

  • results (list[Any])

count: int
next: str | None
previous: str | None
results: list[Any]
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

openviper.middleware

Base

Base middleware infrastructure for OpenViper.

Middlewares are ASGI callables that wrap the application. Each middleware receives the scope, receive, and send parameters from the ASGI spec and can intercept, modify, or short-circuit the request/response cycle.

Example

from openviper.middleware.base import BaseMiddleware
from openviper.http import Request, JSONResponse

class MyMiddleware(BaseMiddleware):
    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        request = Request(scope, receive)
        # ... do something before the view
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        # ... do something after the view (if needed)
class openviper.middleware.base.BaseMiddleware(app)[source]

Bases: object

Base ASGI middleware class.

Subclass this and implement __call__.

Parameters:

app (ASGIApp) – The next ASGI application in the chain.

openviper.middleware.base.build_middleware_stack(app, middleware_classes)[source]

Wrap app with a stack of ASGI middleware in order.

Middlewares are applied inner-first: the first item in the list is the outermost wrapper (first to receive the request).

Parameters:

  • app (Callable[[dict[str, Any], Any, Any], Awaitable[None]]) – The core ASGI application.

  • middleware_classes (list[type[BaseMiddleware] | tuple[type[BaseMiddleware], dict[str, Any]]]) – Ordered list of middleware classes or (cls, kwargs) tuples.

Returns:

A wrapped ASGI app.

Return type:

Callable[[dict[str, Any], Any, Any], Awaitable[None]]

Authentication

Authentication middleware: populates request.user from session or JWT.

class openviper.middleware.auth.AuthenticationMiddleware(app)[source]

Bases: BaseMiddleware

Identify the authenticated user from session cookie or Authorization header.

Sets scope["user"] and scope["auth"] for downstream handlers. If no credentials are present, sets an anonymous user.

Parameters:

app (ASGIApp) – Next ASGI app.

CORS

CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) middleware.

class openviper.middleware.cors.CORSMiddleware(app, allowed_origins=None, allow_credentials=True, allowed_methods=None, allowed_headers=None, expose_headers=None, max_age=600)[source]

Bases: BaseMiddleware

Handle Cross-Origin Resource Sharing headers.

Origin patterns are compiled to re.Pattern objects at __init__ time so no per-request pattern compilation occurs.

Parameters:

  • app (ASGIApp) – Next ASGI app.

  • allowed_origins (list[str] | None) – List of allowed origin strings. Supports wildcards.

  • allow_credentials (bool) – Allow Authorization / cookies cross-origin.

  • allowed_methods (list[str] | None) – Permitted HTTP methods.

  • allowed_headers (list[str] | None) – Permitted request headers (["*"] = all).

  • expose_headers (list[str] | None) – Headers to expose to the browser.

  • max_age (int) – Preflight cache TTL in seconds.

allow_credentials
max_age

CSRF

CSRF protection middleware.

class openviper.middleware.csrf.CSRFMiddleware(app, secret='', cookie_name='csrftoken', header_name='x-csrftoken', exempt_paths=None)[source]

Bases: BaseMiddleware

CSRF protection via double-submit cookie pattern.

Safe methods (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS) pass through without validation. Unsafe methods require a matching X-CSRFToken header or form field.

Parameters:

  • app (ASGIApp) – Next ASGI app.

  • secret (str) – Signing secret (uses settings.SECRET_KEY by default).

  • cookie_name (str) – Name of the CSRF cookie.

  • header_name (str) – Name of the HTTP header to check.

  • exempt_paths (list[str] | None) – Paths that skip CSRF validation.

Rate Limit

Token-bucket / sliding-window rate limiting middleware and decorator.

The sliding window counter uses 256 independent asyncio.Lock objects (one per hash stripe) instead of a single global lock. Under concurrent load this reduces contention by ~256×: only requests from the same hash stripe can contend, which in practice means only 1/256 of key pairs block each other.

class openviper.middleware.ratelimit.RateLimitMiddleware(app, max_requests=None, window_seconds=None, key_func=None)[source]

Bases: BaseMiddleware

ASGI rate limiting middleware using a per-key sliding window.

Settings (can also be passed directly):

RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS = 100   # requests allowed per window
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW   = 60    # window size in seconds
RATE_LIMIT_BY       = "ip"  # "ip" | "user" | "path"

The middleware adds X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and Retry-After headers to every response.

Parameters:

  • app (Any)

  • max_requests (int | None)

  • window_seconds (float | None)

  • key_func (Callable[[dict[str, Any]], str] | None)

max_requests: int
window_seconds: float
counter
openviper.middleware.ratelimit.rate_limit(max_requests=60, window_seconds=60.0)[source]

Decorator to apply per-view rate limiting based on client IP.

Usage:

@app.get("/expensive")
@rate_limit(max_requests=10, window_seconds=60)
async def expensive_view(request: Request):
    ...
Parameters:

  • max_requests (int)

  • window_seconds (float)

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

Security

Security middleware: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, etc.

class openviper.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware(app, ssl_redirect=False, hsts_seconds=0, hsts_include_subdomains=False, hsts_preload=False, x_frame_options='DENY', content_type_nosniff=True, xss_filter=None, csp=None)[source]

Bases: BaseMiddleware

Adds HTTP security headers to all responses.

Headers added:

  • X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

  • X-Frame-Options: DENY (configurable)

  • Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

  • Strict-Transport-Security (when hsts_seconds > 0)

  • Redirect HTTP → HTTPS when ssl_redirect=True

Parameters:

  • app (ASGIApp) – Next ASGI app.

  • ssl_redirect (bool) – Redirect all HTTP requests to HTTPS.

  • hsts_seconds (int) – HSTS max-age. 0 disables the header.

  • hsts_include_subdomains (bool) – Include subdomains in HSTS.

  • hsts_preload (bool) – Add preload directive to HSTS.

  • x_frame_options (str) – DENY, SAMEORIGIN, or empty to disable.

  • content_type_nosniff (bool) – Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.

  • xss_filter (bool | None) – Add X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block.

  • csp (dict[str, Any] | str | None) – Optional Content-Security-Policy dictionary or string.

ssl_redirect
hsts_seconds
hsts_include_subdomains
hsts_preload
x_frame_options
content_type_nosniff
xss_filter
csp

openviper.admin

Registry

Admin registry - manages registered models and their admin configurations.

The AdminRegistry is the central hub for the admin panel. It stores all registered models and their corresponding ModelAdmin configurations.

exception openviper.admin.registry.AlreadyRegistered[source]

Bases: ValueError

Raised when a model is registered more than once.

exception openviper.admin.registry.NotRegistered[source]

Bases: ValueError

Raised when accessing an unregistered model.

class openviper.admin.registry.AdminRegistry[source]

Bases: object

Central registry for admin-managed models.

This class maintains a mapping of model classes to their ModelAdmin configurations and provides methods for registration, discovery, and retrieval.

_registry

Dict mapping model classes to ModelAdmin instances.

_discovered

Whether auto-discovery has been run.

register(model_class, admin_class=None)[source]

Register a model with the admin site.

Can be used as a decorator or called directly.

Parameters:

  • model_class (type[Model]) – The model class to register.

  • admin_class (type[ModelAdmin] | None) – Optional custom ModelAdmin class.

Returns:

When used as a decorator, returns a decorator function. When called directly with admin_class or default, returns None.

Raises:

AlreadyRegistered – If the model is already registered.

Return type:

type[ModelAdmin] | None | callable

unregister(model_class)[source]

Unregister a model from the admin site.

Parameters:

model_class (type[Model]) – The model class to unregister.

Raises:

NotRegistered – If the model is not registered.

Return type:

None

get_model_admin(model_class)[source]

Get the ModelAdmin instance for a model.

Parameters:

model_class (type[Model]) – The model class to look up.

Returns:

The ModelAdmin instance for this model, or None if not registered.

Return type:

ModelAdmin | None

get_model_admin_by_name(model_name)[source]

Get a ModelAdmin instance by model name.

Parameters:

model_name (str) – The model class name (case-insensitive).

Returns:

The ModelAdmin instance.

Raises:

NotRegistered – If no model with that name is registered.

Return type:

ModelAdmin

get_model_by_name(model_name)[source]

Get a model class by name.

Parameters:

model_name (str) – The model class name (case-insensitive).

Returns:

The model class.

Raises:

NotRegistered – If no model with that name is registered.

Return type:

type[Model]

get_all_models()[source]

Get all registered models with their admin configurations.

Returns:

List of (model_class, model_admin) tuples.

Return type:

list[tuple[type[Model], ModelAdmin]]

is_registered(model_class)[source]

Check if a model is registered.

Parameters:

model_class (type[Model]) – The model class to check.

Returns:

True if registered, False otherwise.

bool

discover_from_app(app_name)[source]

Import and register models from an app’s admin.py module.

app_name (str) – Dotted path to the app (e.g., ‘blog’ or ‘apps.blog’).

None

auto_discover_from_installed_apps()[source]

Auto-discover admin.py modules from all INSTALLED_APPS.

This method imports the admin.py module from each installed app, which triggers any @register decorators defined there.

Return type:

None

Get registered models grouped by their app name.

Returns:

Dict mapping app names to lists of (model, admin) tuples.

Return type:

dict[str, list[tuple[type[Model], ModelAdmin]]]

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – The model class name (case-insensitive).

Returns:

The ModelAdmin instance.

Raises:

NotRegistered – If no model with that app/name is registered.

Return type:

ModelAdmin

get_model_by_app_and_name(app_label, model_name)[source]

Get a model class by app label and model name.

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – The model class name (case-insensitive).

Returns:

The model class.

Raises:

NotRegistered – If no model with that app/name is registered.

type[Model]

get_registered_models()[source]

Alias for tests. Returns list of model classes.

list[type[Model]]

Alias for tests. Returns model class or None.

Parameters:

Alias for tests. Returns model info/config.

dict

get_all_model_configs()[source]

Alias for tests. Returns list of all model configs.

Return type:

list[dict]

clear()[source]

Clear all registrations. Primarily for testing.

Return type:

None

Options (ModelAdmin)

ModelAdmin options class - configuration for model admin behavior.

The ModelAdmin class provides configuration options for how models are displayed and edited in the admin panel.

class openviper.admin.options.ModelAdmin(model_class)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration class for admin model behavior.

Subclass this to customize how a model appears in the admin panel.

Example:

class PostAdmin(ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ["title", "author", "created_at", "is_published"]
    list_filter = ["is_published", "created_at"]
    search_fields = ["title", "body"]
    readonly_fields = ["created_at", "updated_at"]
    fieldsets = [
        ("Content", {"fields": ["title", "body"]}),
        ("Meta", {"fields": ["author", "is_published"], "classes": ["collapse"]}),
    ]
Parameters:

model_class (type[Model])

list_display: list[str] = []
list_display_links: list[str] | None = None
list_filter: list[str] = []
list_editable: list[str] = []
search_fields: list[str] = []
ordering: str | list[str] | None = None
list_per_page: int = 25
list_max_show_all: int = 200
date_hierarchy: str | None = None
fields: list[str] | None = None
exclude: list[str] | None = None
readonly_fields: list[str] = []
fieldsets: list[tuple[str | None, dict[str, Any]]] | None = None
form_fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
sensitive_fields: list[str] = ['password']
actions: list[Callable] = []
actions_on_top: bool = True
actions_on_bottom: bool = False
save_on_top: bool = False
preserve_filters: bool = True
show_full_result_count: bool = True
inlines: list[type[InlineModelAdmin]] = []
child_tables: list[type[ChildTable]] = []
__init__(model_class)[source]

Initialize ModelAdmin with the model it manages.

Parameters:

model_class (type[Model]) – The model class this admin manages.

Return type:

None

Get fields to display in list view.

Parameters:

request (Request | None) – The current request (for dynamic display).

List of field names to display.

list[str]

get_fields(request=None, obj=None)[source]

Get fields to show in the form.

Parameters:

  • obj (Model | None) – The object being edited (None for create).

List of field names to show in form.

list[str]

get_exclude(request=None, obj=None)[source]

Get fields to exclude from the form.

Parameters:

  • obj (Model | None) – The object being edited.

List of field names to exclude.

Return type:

list[str]

get_sensitive_fields(request=None, obj=None)[source]

Get fields that should never be serialized/exposed in API responses.

These fields (like password) are excluded from all data responses to prevent exposure of sensitive data.

Parameters:

  • request (Request | None) – The current request.

  • obj (Model | None) – The object being viewed/edited.

Returns:

List of field names that should never be exposed.

Return type:

list[str]

get_readonly_fields(request=None, obj=None)[source]

Get fields that should be read-only.

Parameters:

  • request (Request | None) – The current request.

  • obj (Model | None) – The object being edited.

Returns:

List of field names that cannot be edited.

Return type:

list[str]

get_fieldsets(request=None, obj=None)[source]

Get fieldsets for form grouping.

Parameters:

  • request (Request | None) – The current request.

  • obj (Model | None) – The object being edited.

Returns:

List of (title, options) tuples for field grouping.

Return type:

list[tuple[str | None, dict[str, Any]]]

Get custom configuration for a form field.

Parameters:

field_name (str) – The field to get config for.

Returns:

Dict of field widget configuration.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Bases: object
