Parameters:

parent_model (type[Model])

Decorators

Admin decorators for model registration.

Provides the @register decorator for registering models with the admin site.

openviper.admin.decorators.register(*models)[source]

Decorator to register one or more models with the admin site.

Can register multiple models with the same ModelAdmin class.

Parameters:

*models (type[Model]) – One or more model classes to register.

Returns:

A decorator that registers the ModelAdmin class.

Return type:

Callable[[type[ModelAdmin]], type[ModelAdmin]]

Example

@register(Post)
class PostAdmin(ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ["title", "created_at"]

# Multiple models with same admin
@register(Post, Article)
class ContentAdmin(ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ["title"]

Actions

Batch actions system for admin panel.

Provides infrastructure for defining and executing bulk operations on selected objects in the admin list view.

class openviper.admin.actions.ActionResult(success, count, message, errors=None)[source]

Bases: object

Result of executing an admin action.

Parameters:

  • success (bool)

  • count (int)

  • message (str)

  • errors (list[str] | None)

success: bool

Whether the action completed successfully.

count: int

Number of objects affected.

message: str

Human-readable result message.

errors: list[str] | None = None

List of error messages if any.

class openviper.admin.actions.AdminAction[source]

Bases: object

Base class for admin batch actions.

Subclass this to create custom actions that can be performed on multiple selected objects.

confirm_message: str | None = None

Optional confirmation prompt.

permissions: list[str] = []

List of required permissions.

name: str = ''

Internal name for the action.

description: str = ''

Human-readable description shown in UI.

async execute(queryset, request, model_admin=None)[source]

Execute the action on the queryset.

Parameters:

  • queryset (QuerySet) – QuerySet of selected objects.

  • request (Request) – The current request.

  • model_admin (ModelAdmin | None) – Optional ModelAdmin instance.

Returns:

ActionResult with success status and message.

Return type:

ActionResult

has_permission(request)[source]

Check if the user has permission to run this action.

Parameters:

request (Request) – The current request.

Returns:

True if user can execute this action.

Return type:

bool

get_info()[source]

Get action metadata for API response.

Returns:

Dict with action info.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

class openviper.admin.actions.DeleteSelectedAction[source]

Bases: AdminAction

Built-in action to delete selected objects.

name: str = 'delete_selected'

Internal name for the action.

description: str = 'Delete selected items'

Human-readable description shown in UI.

confirm_message: str | None = 'Are you sure you want to delete the selected items? This action cannot be undone.'

Optional confirmation prompt.

async execute(queryset, request, model_admin=None)[source]

Delete all objects in the queryset.

Parameters:

  • queryset (QuerySet) – QuerySet of objects to delete.

  • request (Request) – The current request.

  • model_admin (ModelAdmin | None) – Optional ModelAdmin instance.

Returns:

ActionResult with count of deleted objects.

Return type:

ActionResult

openviper.admin.actions.register_action(action_class)[source]

Register a custom action with the admin system.

Parameters:

action_class (type[AdminAction]) – The AdminAction subclass to register.

Returns:

The same class (for use as decorator).

Return type:

type[AdminAction]

openviper.admin.actions.get_action(name)[source]

Get an action instance by name.

Parameters:

name (str) – The action name.

Returns:

AdminAction instance or None if not found.

Return type:

AdminAction | None

openviper.admin.actions.get_available_actions(request)[source]

Get all actions available to the current user.

Parameters:

request (Request) – The current request.

Returns:

List of AdminAction instances the user can execute.

Return type:

list[AdminAction]

openviper.admin.actions.action(description=None, confirm_message=None, permissions=None)[source]

Decorator to create an action from a function.

Parameters:

  • description (str | None) – Human-readable description.

  • confirm_message (str | None) – Optional confirmation prompt.

  • permissions (list[str] | None) – Required permissions.

Returns:

Decorator function.

Return type:

Callable

Example

@action(description="Mark as published", confirm_message="Publish selected posts?")
async def publish_selected(queryset, request):
    count = await queryset.update(is_published=True)
    return ActionResult(success=True, count=count, message=f"Published {count} posts")

History

Change history tracking for admin panel.

Provides audit logging for all create, update, and delete operations performed through the admin interface.

class openviper.admin.history.ChangeAction(*values)[source]

Bases: StrEnum

Type of change made to an object.

ADD = 'add'
CHANGE = 'change'
DELETE = 'delete'
class openviper.admin.history.ChangeHistory(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model for tracking changes to admin-managed objects.

Stores a record of every create, update, and delete operation performed through the admin interface.

Parameters:

kwargs (Any)

model_name = CharField(name='model_name')
object_id = IntegerField(name='object_id')
object_repr = CharField(name='object_repr')
action = CharField(name='action')
changed_fields = TextField(name='changed_fields')
changed_by_id = IntegerField(name='changed_by_id')
changed_by_username = CharField(name='changed_by_username')
change_time = DateTimeField(name='change_time')
change_message = TextField(name='change_message')
class Meta[source]

Bases: object

table_name = 'admin_changehistory'
get_changed_fields_dict()[source]

Parse changed_fields JSON to dict.

Returns:

Dict of field changes.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

classmethod get_for_object(model_name, object_id, limit=50)[source]

Get change history for a specific object.

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – Name of the model.

  • object_id (int) – ID of the object.

  • limit (int) – Maximum records to return.

Returns:

List of ChangeHistory records.

Return type:

list[ChangeHistory]

objects: ClassVar[Manager] = Manager(model=ChangeHistory)

The default Manager for query building.

async openviper.admin.history.log_change(model_name, object_id, action, changes=None, user=None, object_repr=None, message=None)[source]

Log a change to the history.

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – Name of the model that was changed.

  • object_id (int) – ID of the object that was changed.

  • action (ChangeAction | str) – Type of change (add, change, delete).

  • changes (dict[str, Any] | None) – Dict of field changes (old -> new values).

  • user (Any) – The user who made the change.

  • object_repr (str | None) – String representation of the object.

  • message (str | None) – Optional change message.

Returns:

The created ChangeHistory record.

Return type:

ChangeHistory

async openviper.admin.history.get_change_history(model_name, object_id, limit=50)[source]

Get change history for an object.

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – Name of the model.

  • object_id (int) – ID of the object.

  • limit (int) – Maximum records to return.

Returns:

List of ChangeHistory records, most recent first.

Return type:

list[ChangeHistory]

async openviper.admin.history.get_recent_activity(limit=20)[source]

Get recent change activity across all models.

Parameters:

limit (int) – Maximum records to return.

Returns:

List of recent ChangeHistory records.

Return type:

list[ChangeHistory]

openviper.admin.history.compute_changes(old_data, new_data)[source]

Compute the differences between old and new data.

Parameters:

  • old_data (dict[str, Any]) – Previous field values.

  • new_data (dict[str, Any]) – New field values.

Returns:

value, “new”: value}.

Return type:

Dict mapping field names to {“old”

openviper.tasks

Core

Core Scheduler class for OpenViper’s periodic task system.

Scheduler is the entry point for application code. Register tasks with add() for periodic execution, or call run_now() to enqueue a task immediately without any schedule.

Examples:

from openviper.tasks.core import Scheduler
from openviper.tasks.schedule import CronSchedule, IntervalSchedule

scheduler = Scheduler()

# Periodic task with arguments
@task(queue_name="default")
async def send_report(user_id: int, fmt: str = "pdf") -> None: ...

scheduler.add(
    "weekly_report",
    send_report,
    CronSchedule("0 8 * * 1"),   # every Monday 08:00
    args=(42,),
    kwargs={"fmt": "csv"},
)

# One-shot instant job — enqueue right now, no schedule
scheduler.run_now(send_report, 42, fmt="html")

# Periodic loop:
enqueued = scheduler.tick()   # returns list of names that were sent
class openviper.tasks.core.Scheduler(registry=None)[source]

Bases: object

Periodic task scheduler backed by a ScheduleRegistry.

Parameters:

registry (ScheduleRegistry | None) – A custom registry instance. Uses the process-level singleton when None (the default).

add(name, actor, schedule, *, args=(), kwargs=None, enabled=True, replace=False)[source]

Register a new periodic task.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Unique name for the scheduled entry.

  • actor (Any) – A Dramatiq actor (decorated with @task).

  • schedule (Schedule) – When to enqueue — CronSchedule or IntervalSchedule.

  • args (tuple[Any, ...]) – Positional args forwarded to actor.send().

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None) – Keyword args forwarded to actor.send().

  • enabled (bool) – Set to False to register but suppress enqueuing.

  • replace (bool) – Overwrite an existing entry with the same name.

Returns:

The newly created ScheduleEntry.

Return type:

ScheduleEntry

remove(name)[source]

Unregister the entry named name. No-op if not found.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

None

run_now(actor, /, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Enqueue actor immediately — a one-shot fire-and-forget job.

Unlike add(), this does not register a recurring entry in the registry. The message is sent to the Dramatiq broker right away and result tracking is handled by the existing TaskTrackingMiddleware.

Parameters:

  • actor (Any) – A Dramatiq actor (decorated with @task).

  • *args (Any) – Positional arguments forwarded to actor.send().

  • **kwargs (Any) – Keyword arguments forwarded to actor.send().

Raises:

Exception – Re-raises any broker error so the caller can handle it.

Return type:

None

Example:

scheduler.run_now(send_report, user_id=42, fmt="pdf")
tick(now=None)[source]

Enqueue all tasks whose schedule is currently due.

Each due entry has its actor called with actor.send(*args, **kwargs) and its last_run_at updated to now so it is not re-enqueued until the next trigger time.

Parameters:

now (datetime | None) – Reference UTC datetime. Defaults to datetime.now(timezone.utc) when None.

Returns:

Sorted list of entry names that were successfully enqueued.

Return type:

list[str]

get_registry()[source]

Return the underlying ScheduleRegistry.

Return type:

ScheduleRegistry

all_entries()[source]

Return all registered ScheduleEntry objects.

Return type:

list[ScheduleEntry]

Decorators

@task decorator — registers a function as a Dramatiq actor.

Usage:

from openviper.tasks import task

@task(queue_name="emails", max_retries=5)
async def send_welcome(user_id: int) -> None:
    ...

# Enqueue
send_welcome.send(42)       # fire-and-forget
send_welcome.delay(42)      # alias for .send()
send_welcome.send_with_options(args=(42,), delay=5_000)  # 5-second delay

# Explicit actor name (avoids collisions when two apps share a function name)
@task(actor_name="payments.charge")
async def charge(order_id: int) -> None:
    ...

Async functions are handled transparently by Dramatiq’s built-in AsyncIO middleware, which the broker attaches in openviper.tasks.broker. No manual event-loop wiring is needed.

openviper.tasks.decorators.task(queue_name='default', priority=0, max_retries=3, min_backoff=15000, max_backoff=300000, time_limit=None, actor_name=None)[source]

Decorator that registers a callable as a Dramatiq background task.

Parameters:

  • queue_name (str) – Queue to route the message to. Workers can be restricted to specific queues with --queues.

  • priority (int) – Message priority within the queue (higher = processed sooner when the queue is longer than the concurrency).

  • max_retries (int) – Maximum number of automatic retries on failure. Set to 0 to disable retries.

  • min_backoff (int) – Minimum retry back-off in milliseconds.

  • max_backoff (int) – Maximum retry back-off in milliseconds.

  • time_limit (int | None) – Hard execution timeout in milliseconds, or None for no limit.

  • actor_name (str | None) – Explicit Dramatiq actor name. Defaults to the function’s own __name__ (e.g. "moderate"). Override this when two apps define functions with the same name to avoid registration collisions.

Returns:

A decorated Dramatiq Actor with an extra .delay() alias on .send().

Return type:

Callable[[…], Any]

Schedule

Schedule descriptors for OpenViper’s periodic task system.

Two concrete schedule types are provided:

  • CronSchedule — parses a five-field cron expression ("*/5 * * * *"). Uses croniter when installed; falls back to a minimal built-in evaluator for simple patterns.

  • IntervalSchedule — fires every N seconds.

Both implement the Schedule protocol so that the scheduler loop can call schedule.is_due(last_run_at, now) uniformly.

Usage:

from openviper.tasks.schedule import CronSchedule, IntervalSchedule

every_minute = CronSchedule("* * * * *")
every_5s     = IntervalSchedule(5)
class openviper.tasks.schedule.Schedule[source]

Bases: ABC

Protocol / abstract base class for all schedule descriptors.

abstractmethod is_due(last_run_at, now=None)[source]

Return True if the task should be enqueued right now.

Parameters:

  • last_run_at (datetime | None) – The UTC datetime of the most recent successful enqueue, or None if the task has never run.

  • now (datetime | None) – The current UTC datetime. Defaults to datetime.now(timezone.utc) when None.

Return type:

bool

class openviper.tasks.schedule.CronSchedule(expr, *, use_seconds=False)[source]

Bases: Schedule

Fire according to a standard five-field cron expression.

Attempts to use croniter (pip install croniter) for full cron semantics including special strings like @hourly. Falls back to a built-in evaluator for simpler patterns when croniter is not installed.

Parameters:

  • expr (str) – Five-field cron expression, e.g. "*/5 * * * *".

  • use_seconds (bool) – Reserved; currently ignored (minute-granularity only).

Raises:

ValueError – If expr cannot be parsed.

Example:

CronSchedule("0 * * * *")     # top of every hour
CronSchedule("*/15 * * * *")  # every 15 minutes
is_due(last_run_at, now=None)[source]

Return True if the cron expression matches the current minute.

Uses croniter when available; otherwise falls back to the built-in evaluator. Note: this checks whether now falls within the cron period — call tick() at most once per minute to avoid duplicate enqueues.

Parameters:

  • last_run_at (datetime | None)

  • now (datetime | None)

Return type:

bool

class openviper.tasks.schedule.IntervalSchedule(seconds)[source]

Bases: Schedule

Fire every seconds seconds.

Parameters:

seconds (float) – Minimum number of seconds between consecutive enqueues. Must be a positive number.

Raises:

ValueError – If seconds is not positive.

Example:

IntervalSchedule(60)    # every minute
IntervalSchedule(3600)  # every hour
is_due(last_run_at, now=None)[source]

Return True when at least seconds have elapsed since last_run_at.

Parameters:

  • last_run_at (datetime | None)

  • now (datetime | None)

Return type:

bool

Scheduler

Periodic task scheduler — integrates with openviper’s built-in scheduler.

Uses openviper.tasks.core.Scheduler (CronSchedule / IntervalSchedule) to fire @task-decorated actors on a schedule. The scheduler ticks in a daemon thread inside the worker process — no separate beat process or third-party library is required.

Usage

@periodic automatically applies @task() when the decorated function is not already a Dramatiq actor, so the simple form just works:

from openviper.tasks import periodic

@periodic(every=300)           # every 5 minutes
async def sync_feeds():
    ...

@periodic(cron="0 8 * * 1-5")  # weekdays at 08:00
async def morning_report():
    ...

Stack @task() explicitly only when you need Dramatiq-specific options such as a custom queue, retry policy, or time limit:

from openviper.tasks import task, periodic

@periodic(every=3600, run_on_start=True)
@task(queue_name="maintenance", time_limit=30_000)
async def purge_tmp_files():
    ...

@periodic(every=60, args=(42,), kwargs={"dry_run": True})
@task()
async def poll(user_id: int, *, dry_run: bool = False):
    ...
openviper.tasks.scheduler.periodic(every=None, cron=None, *, run_on_start=False, name=None, args=(), kwargs=None)[source]

Schedule a @task-decorated actor to run automatically.

If the decorated function is not already a Dramatiq actor, @task() is applied automatically with default options. Stack @task() explicitly only when you need custom queue, retry, or time-limit settings.

Parameters:

  • every (int | float | None) – Interval in seconds between consecutive runs.

  • cron (str | None) – Five-field cron expression ("minute hour dom month dow").

  • run_on_start (bool) – Enqueue the task once immediately when the worker starts before the first scheduled tick.

  • name (str | None) – Registry name for this entry. Defaults to the actor’s actor_name. Must be unique across all @periodic registrations.

  • args (tuple[Any, ...]) – Positional arguments forwarded to actor.send().

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None) – Keyword arguments forwarded to actor.send().

Raises:

ValueError – If neither every nor cron is supplied.

Return type:

Any

openviper.tasks.scheduler.start_scheduler()[source]

Register all pending entries and start the tick thread.

Called by openviper.tasks.worker.run_worker() after the Dramatiq worker has started. Safe to call when the registry is empty.

Return type:

None

openviper.tasks.scheduler.stop_scheduler()[source]

Stop the tick thread.

Called by openviper.tasks.worker.run_worker() on shutdown.

Return type:

None

openviper.tasks.scheduler.reset_scheduler()[source]

Stop the scheduler and clear all pending registrations.

Primarily for tests — call this in teardown to ensure a clean slate between test cases that register @periodic tasks.

Return type:

None

Registry

Schedule entry registry for OpenViper’s periodic task scheduler.

Each registered task is stored as a ScheduleEntry. The ScheduleRegistry singleton keeps track of all entries and answers which entries are due right now?

Usage:

from openviper.tasks.registry import get_registry

registry = get_registry()
registry.register(
    name="send_digest",
    actor=send_digest_actor,
    schedule=CronSchedule("0 8 * * *"),
)
due = registry.all_due(now=datetime.now(timezone.utc))
class openviper.tasks.registry.ScheduleEntry(name, actor, schedule, args=<factory>, kwargs=<factory>, enabled=True, last_run_at=None)[source]

Bases: object

Holds all state for one scheduled task.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • actor (Any)

  • schedule (Schedule)

  • args (tuple[Any, ...])

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any])

  • enabled (bool)

  • last_run_at (datetime | None)

name: str

Unique human-readable identifier.

actor: Any

The Dramatiq actor (registered via @task).

schedule: Schedule

A Schedule instance.

args: tuple[Any, ...]

Positional arguments forwarded to actor.send().

kwargs: dict[str, Any]

Keyword arguments forwarded to actor.send().

enabled: bool = True

When False the entry is never enqueued.

last_run_at: datetime | None = None

UTC datetime of the most recent enqueue; None initially.

is_due(now=None)[source]

Delegate to self.schedule.is_due if the entry is enabled.

Parameters:

now (datetime | None)

Return type:

bool

class openviper.tasks.registry.ScheduleRegistry[source]

Bases: object

In-process store of ScheduleEntry objects.

Use get_registry() to obtain the process-level singleton.

register(name, actor, schedule, *, args=(), kwargs=None, enabled=True, replace=False)[source]

Add a new ScheduleEntry to the registry.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Unique identifier. A second call with the same name raises ValueError unless replace is True.

  • actor (Any) – Dramatiq actor to enqueue.

  • schedule (Schedule) – Schedule descriptor.

  • args (tuple[Any, ...]) – Positional arguments for the actor.

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any] | None) – Keyword arguments for the actor.

  • enabled (bool) – Whether the entry participates in scheduling.

  • replace (bool) – If True, silently overwrite an existing entry with the same name instead of raising.

Returns:

The newly created ScheduleEntry.

Raises:

ValueError – If name is already registered and replace is False.

Return type:

ScheduleEntry

unregister(name)[source]

Remove the entry named name. No-op if not found.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

None

get(name)[source]

Return the entry with name, or None.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

ScheduleEntry | None

all_entries()[source]

Return all registered entries.

Return type:

list[ScheduleEntry]

all_due(now=None)[source]

Return entries whose schedule is currently due.

Parameters:

now (datetime | None) – The reference datetime (UTC). Defaults to datetime.now(timezone.utc).

Return type:

list[ScheduleEntry]

clear()[source]

Remove all entries. Primarily for tests.

Return type:

None

openviper.tasks.registry.get_registry()[source]

Return the process-level ScheduleRegistry singleton.

Return type:

ScheduleRegistry

Events

Model event dispatcher for OpenViper tasks.

Allows per-model lifecycle event hooks to be declared in project settings via MODEL_EVENTS and automatically fired from Model save() / delete() calls.

The dispatcher is only active when TASKS['enabled'] evaluates to True. When the task system is disabled get_dispatcher() returns None and Model._trigger_event() becomes a no-op.

Settings

MODEL_EVENTS = {
    "blog.models.Post": {
        "before_validate": ["blog.events.sanitise_post"],
        "before_save":     ["blog.events.stamp_updated_at"],
        "after_insert":    ["blog.events.create_likes"],
        "on_change":       ["blog.events.reindex_post"],
        "after_delete":    ["blog.events.cleanup_comments"],
    },
    "blog.models.Comment": {
        "after_insert": ["blog.events.notify_post_author"],
    },
}

Model paths are matched against "{module}.{ClassName}" of the model instance. Handler dotted-paths are resolved once at dispatcher construction time, so runtime dispatch is a pair of O(1) dict lookups.

Supported event names

All nine Model lifecycle hooks are supported.

save() — create flow (pk is None):

before_validate

Fired before field validation on INSERT.

validate

Fired after built-in field validation passes on INSERT.

before_insert

Fired after validation, before the INSERT (create only).

before_save

Fired immediately before the DB write (both create and update).

after_insert

Fired after a successful INSERT (create only).

on_change

Fired after INSERT (all fields treated as changed) or after UPDATE when at least one field value actually changed.

save() — update flow (pk set):

before_validate

Fired before field validation on UPDATE.

validate

Fired after built-in field validation passes on UPDATE.

before_save

Fired immediately before the UPDATE.

on_update

Fired after a successful UPDATE (update only).

on_change

Fired after UPDATE when at least one field value changed.

delete() flow:

on_delete

Fired before the DELETE is issued. Raising here aborts the deletion.

after_delete

Fired after a successful DELETE.

Handler signature

Handlers receive the model instance as their first positional argument, the triggering event name as the event keyword argument, and any extra keyword arguments forwarded via trigger():

def create_likes(post, event: str | None = None) -> None:
    init_like_counter.send(post_id=post.pk)

Handlers are called synchronously (inline with the DB operation) and exceptions are caught and logged so a misbehaving handler never interrupts model persistence or other handlers in the same event.

class openviper.tasks.events.ModelEventDispatcher(config)[source]

Bases: object

Registry of per-model event → handler callables.

Handlers are resolved from dotted-path strings at construction time and stored as {model_path: {event_name: [callable, ...]}}. Runtime dispatch is two O(1) dict lookups — no import overhead.

The instance is immutable after __init__, so concurrent reads require no locking.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

__init__(config)[source]

Build the dispatcher from a MODEL_EVENTS config dict.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any]) – Mapping of model path → {event_name → [dotted_callable]}. Unknown event names are accepted but generate a debug log.

Return type:

None

trigger(model_path, event_name, instance, **kwargs)[source]

Dispatch event_name to all handlers registered for model_path.

Each handler is called with (instance, event=event_name, **kwargs). Exceptions are caught per-handler and logged; they never propagate to the caller.

Parameters:

  • model_path (str) – Dotted "module.ClassName" key (e.g. "blog.models.Post").

  • event_name (str) – Event to dispatch ("after_insert", "on_change", "after_delete").

  • instance (Any) – The model instance that triggered the event.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Extra context forwarded verbatim to every handler.

Return type:

None

openviper.tasks.events.get_dispatcher()[source]

Return the active ModelEventDispatcher, or None.

The dispatcher is initialised lazily on the first call and cached in a module-level variable. Concurrent calls are serialised by _init_lock with a lock-free fast path.

Returns:

None when the task system is disabled (TASKS['enabled'] is falsy) or MODEL_EVENTS is empty.

Return type:

ModelEventDispatcher | None

openviper.tasks.events.reset_dispatcher()[source]

Clear the cached dispatcher singleton.

Call in test teardown (e.g. autouse fixture) to ensure every test case builds a fresh dispatcher from the settings that are active at that moment.

Return type:

None

openviper.tasks.events.model_event: _ModelEventProxy = <openviper.tasks.events._ModelEventProxy object>

Singleton proxy for decorator-based model event registration. See _ModelEventProxy for usage.

Broker

Broker factory for the openviper task system.

Supported backends (set via TASKS["broker"] in project settings):

  • "redis" — dramatiq.brokers.redis.RedisBroker (default)

  • "rabbitmq" — dramatiq.brokers.rabbitmq.RabbitmqBroker

  • "stub" — dramatiq.brokers.stub.StubBroker (testing only)

Full settings example:

import os
from typing import Any

TASKS: dict[str, Any] = {
    "enabled": 1,                  # required — worker will not start without this
    "broker": "redis",
    "broker_url": os.environ.get("REDIS_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/0"),
    "backend_url": os.environ.get("REDIS_BACKEND_URL", "redis://localhost:6379/1"),
    "scheduler_enabled": 1,        # 1/0 or True/False
    "tracking_enabled": 1,         # 1/0 or True/False
    "log_level": "DEBUG",          # optional — falls back to LOG_LEVEL
    "log_format": "json",          # optional — "text" (default) or "json"
    "log_to_file": 1,              # 1/0 or True/False
    "log_dir": "/var/log/myapp",   # optional — defaults to {cwd}/logs
}
backend_url — Redis URL for Dramatiq’s native result backend, which lets

callers retrieve an actor’s return value via message.get_result(). Requires dramatiq[redis]. Independent of the DB-based TaskTrackingMiddleware.

The broker is created once and cached for the lifetime of the process. Call reset_broker() to tear it down (primarily for testing).

openviper.tasks.broker.get_broker()[source]

Return the process-level broker, creating it on first call.

Uses a double-checked lock pattern. A local snapshot of the global is returned so the caller is never exposed to a None value that could result from a concurrent reset_broker() call landing between the lock release and the return statement.

Return type:

Any

openviper.tasks.broker.setup_broker()

Return the process-level broker, creating it on first call.

Uses a double-checked lock pattern. A local snapshot of the global is returned so the caller is never exposed to a None value that could result from a concurrent reset_broker() call landing between the lock release and the return statement.

Return type:

Any

openviper.tasks.broker.reset_broker()[source]

Tear down and forget the current broker. Primarily for tests.

Return type:

None

Worker

In-process Dramatiq worker for openviper.

run_worker() is the single entry-point called by the runworker management command. It:

  1. Sets up file logging (logs/worker.log).

  2. Discovers and imports all task modules from INSTALLED_APPS so that actor registrations happen before the broker is used.

  3. Initialises the broker (Redis / RabbitMQ / Stub).

  4. Starts a dramatiq.Worker in the current process.

  5. Blocks until SIGINT / SIGTERM, then shuts down cleanly.

There is no subprocess. The worker runs entirely in-process, which makes debugging straightforward and avoids the PYTHONPATH / settings propagation issues that a subprocess approach introduces.

Discovery strategy

For each app in INSTALLED_APPS (skipping openviper.* internals) the worker walks the app directory and imports every .py file that is not __init__.py and not inside migrations/, tests/, or __pycache__. Actors registered by @task decorators during those imports are automatically picked up by the broker.

openviper.tasks.worker.discover_tasks(extra_modules=None)[source]

Import task modules from every app in INSTALLED_APPS.

Parameters:

extra_modules (list[str] | None) – Additional dotted module paths to import on top of the auto-discovered ones.

Returns:

Sorted list of module paths that were successfully imported.

Return type:

list[str]

openviper.tasks.worker.create_worker(threads=8, queues=None, extra_modules=None)[source]

Initialize and return a Dramatiq worker instance.

Parameters:

  • threads (int) – Number of worker threads to spawn.

  • queues (list[str] | None) – Restrict processing to these queue names.

  • extra_modules (list[str] | None) – Extra Python modules to import.

Return type:

Worker

openviper.tasks.worker.run_worker(processes=1, threads=8, queues=None, extra_modules=None)[source]

Start the Dramatiq worker and block until a shutdown signal.

Parameters:

  • processes (int) – Number of worker processes (in-process mode uses 1).

  • threads (int) – Number of worker threads to spawn.

  • queues (list[str] | None) – Restrict processing to these queue names. None means consume from all declared queues.

  • extra_modules (list[str] | None) – Extra Python modules to import in addition to those found by auto-discovery.

Return type:

None

openviper.ai

Base Provider

AI model provider base class and inference protocol.

class openviper.ai.base.AIProvider(config)[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for AI model providers.

Implement generate() (and optionally stream()) to integrate a new AI backend with OpenViper.

Example

from openviper.ai.base import AIProvider

class MyProvider(AIProvider):
    name = "myprovider"

    async def generate(self, prompt: str, **kwargs) -> str:
        return "Hello from MyProvider"
Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

name: str = 'base'
abstractmethod async generate(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Generate a text completion for the given prompt.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Provider-specific options (temperature, max_tokens, etc.).

Returns:

Generated text string.

Return type:

str

async stream(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Stream completion tokens.

Default implementation calls generate() and yields the full result as a single chunk. Override for true token-by-token streaming.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Provider-specific options.

Yields:

Incremental text chunks.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

async moderate(content, **kwargs)[source]

Classify content for moderation.

Default implementation sends a structured JSON prompt to generate(). Providers may override for a native moderation API.

Parameters:

  • content (str) – Text to classify.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Forwarded to generate().

Returns:

Dict with keys classification (str), confidence (float 0-1), reason (str), and is_safe (bool).

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

supported_models()[source]

Return the list of model IDs this provider can serve.

Extracts unique model ID values (not display-name keys) from the models and model dicts in the provider config.

Returns:

Sorted list of model ID strings.

Return type:

list[str]

provider_name()[source]

Return the canonical name for this provider (same as the name class attribute).

Return type:

str

async embed(text, **kwargs)[source]

Return an embedding vector for the given text.

Not all providers support embeddings.

Raises:

NotImplementedError – Provider does not support embeddings.

Parameters:

  • text (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

list[float]

async before_inference(prompt, kwargs)[source]

Hook called before each inference. Override to transform input.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – The input prompt.

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any]) – Inference keyword arguments.

Returns:

(modified_prompt, modified_kwargs)

Return type:

tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]

async after_inference(prompt, response)[source]

Hook called after each inference. Override to transform output.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Original prompt.

  • response (str) – Generated response.

Returns:

Modified response.

Return type:

str

async complete(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Alias for generate(). Kept for backward compatibility.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

str

async stream_complete(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Alias for stream(). Kept for backward compatibility.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

Registry

AI model registry — register and retrieve named providers.

ProviderRegistry (primary)

Model-centric: look up by model ID for O(1) routing. Auto-populated from settings.AI_PROVIDERS on first access. Supports external registration via modules, plugin directories, and package entry-points. All public reads are protected by a reentrant lock.

ai_registry (legacy shim)

Deprecated singleton that forwards attribute access to provider_registry with a DeprecationWarning. Will be removed in a future release.

Example:

from openviper.ai.registry import provider_registry

provider = provider_registry.get_by_model("gemini-2.0-flash")
response = await provider.generate("Hello!")

External provider registration:

# From a module
provider_registry.register_from_module("mypackage.ai.my_provider")

# From a directory of provider modules
provider_registry.load_plugins("/path/to/providers/")

# From installed package entry-points
provider_registry.discover_entrypoints()
class openviper.ai.registry.ProviderConfig(provider_type, api_key='', model='', models=(), base_url='', extra=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Immutable configuration record for a single AI provider entry.

Produced from a settings.AI_PROVIDERS dict entry and passed to the provider constructor. Frozen so it can be used as a cache key.

Parameters:

  • provider_type (str)

  • api_key (str)

  • model (str)

  • models (tuple[str, ...])

  • base_url (str)

  • extra (dict[str, Any])

provider_type: str
api_key: str
model: str
models: tuple[str, ...]
base_url: str
extra: dict[str, Any]
class openviper.ai.registry.ProviderRegistry[source]

Bases: object

Thread-safe registry mapping model IDs to provider instances.

Populated automatically from settings.AI_PROVIDERS on first access (double-checked locking ensures this happens exactly once per process).

Third-party providers can be registered without touching core code:

# Programmatic registration
from openviper.ai.registry import provider_registry
provider_registry.register_provider(MyProvider({"model": "my-model"}))

# From a module that exposes get_providers() or PROVIDERS
provider_registry.register_from_module("mypackage.ai.my_provider")

# From a directory of .py provider modules
provider_registry.load_plugins("/etc/myapp/ai_plugins/")

# From installed packages that declare an entry-point
provider_registry.discover_entrypoints()  # group="openviper.ai.providers"
ENTRYPOINT_GROUP = 'openviper.ai.providers'

Entry-point group scanned by discover_entrypoints().

register_provider(provider, *, allow_override=True)[source]

Register all models exposed by provider.

Calls provider.supported_models() and maps every returned model ID to provider.

Parameters:

  • provider (AIProvider) – A fully-initialised AIProvider.

  • allow_override (bool) – When True (default) an existing mapping for the same model ID is silently replaced with a warning log. Set to False to raise ModelCollisionError instead.

Raises:

ModelCollisionError – allow_override is False and another provider already owns one of the model IDs.

Return type:

None

register_from_module(module_path, *, allow_override=True)[source]

Import module_path and register any providers it exports.

The module is expected to expose providers through either:

  • A callable named get_providers that returns list[AIProvider], or

  • A module-level variable named PROVIDERS of type list[AIProvider].

Both conventions are checked; get_providers() takes precedence.

Parameters:

  • module_path (str) – Dotted Python module path, e.g. "mypackage.ai.my_provider".

  • allow_override (bool) – Forwarded to register_provider().

Returns:

Number of provider instances that were registered.

Raises:

  • ImportError – The module could not be imported.

  • ModelCollisionError – Collision detected and allow_override is False.

Return type:

int

load_plugins(plugin_dir, *, allow_override=True)[source]

Walk plugin_dir and register providers found in each .py file.

Each .py file is imported as a top-level module using its stem as the module name. Files that do not expose a get_providers function or PROVIDERS variable are silently skipped.

Parameters:

  • plugin_dir (str) – Filesystem path to the directory containing plugin modules.

  • allow_override (bool) – Forwarded to register_provider().

Returns:

Total number of provider instances that were registered across all plugin modules.

Raises:

ModelCollisionError – Collision detected and allow_override is False.

Return type:

int

discover_entrypoints(group='openviper.ai.providers', *, allow_override=True)[source]

Register providers declared via package entry-points.

Third-party packages can advertise providers by declaring an entry-point in their pyproject.toml:

[project.entry-points."openviper.ai.providers"]
my_provider = "mypackage.ai.my_provider:get_providers"

Each entry-point value must be a callable that returns either a single AIProvider instance or a list of them.

Parameters:

  • group (str) – Entry-point group name (default: "openviper.ai.providers").

  • allow_override (bool) – Forwarded to register_provider().

Returns:

Total number of provider instances that were registered.

Return type:

int

get_by_model(model_id)[source]

Return the provider registered for model_id.

Auto-loads from settings on first call.

Parameters:

model_id (str) – Exact model identifier (e.g. "gpt-4o").

Returns:

AIProvider instance.

Raises:

ModelNotFoundError – No provider is registered for model_id.

Return type:

AIProvider

list_models()[source]

Return all registered model IDs (sorted).

Return type:

list[str]

list_provider_names()[source]

Return the unique provider names that have been registered.

Return type:

list[str]

reset()[source]

Clear all registrations and force a reload on next access (for tests).

Return type:

None

openviper.ai.registry.ai_registry = <openviper.ai.registry._LegacyAIRegistry object>

Deprecated; kept for one release cycle. Use provider_registry.

Exceptions

Exceptions for the openviper AI subsystem.

ModelNotFoundError and ModelCollisionError have been promoted to the top-level openviper.exceptions module and are re-exported here for convenience.

openviper.ai.exceptions.AIError

alias of AIException

exception openviper.ai.exceptions.AIException[source]

Bases: OpenViperException

Base exception for all OpenViper AI errors.

exception openviper.ai.exceptions.ModelUnavailableError(model, provider, reason='')[source]

Bases: AIException

Raised when a model is registered but the underlying provider cannot serve it.

Parameters:

  • model (str)

  • provider (str)

  • reason (str)

Return type:

None

model
provider
exception openviper.ai.exceptions.ProviderNotAvailableError(provider, reason='')[source]

Bases: AIException

Raised when a provider cannot be initialised (e.g. missing SDK or bad API key).

Parameters:

  • provider (str)

  • reason (str)

Return type:

None

provider
exception openviper.ai.exceptions.ProviderNotConfiguredError(provider)[source]

Bases: AIException

Raised when a provider type is listed in settings but has no usable configuration.

Parameters:

provider (str)

Return type:

None

provider

Extension

Stable public API for third-party openviper AI provider authors.

Import everything you need from this single module. The symbols exported here follow semantic versioning — breaking changes will increment EXTENSION_API_VERSION.

Quickstart:

from openviper.ai.extension import (
    AIProvider,
    provider_registry,
    ModelCollisionError,
    EXTENSION_API_VERSION,
)
import asyncio
from typing import Any


class MyProvider(AIProvider):
    name = "my_provider"

    async def generate(self, prompt: str, **kwargs: Any) -> str:
        # Call your inference backend here
        return f"[MyProvider] {prompt}"


def get_providers() -> list[AIProvider]:
    return [MyProvider({"models": {"My Model": "my-model-v1"}})]


# Then register at app startup:
#   provider_registry.register_from_module("mypackage.ai.my_provider")
# Or via package entry-point in pyproject.toml:
#   [project.entry-points."openviper.ai.providers"]
#   my_provider = "mypackage.ai.my_provider:get_providers"

Versioning guarantee

The EXTENSION_API_VERSION constant is a (major, minor) tuple.

  • Minor bumps add new optional methods / helpers without breaking existing providers.

  • Major bumps indicate breaking interface changes; check the changelog.

Current version: (1, 0).

openviper.ai.extension.EXTENSION_API_VERSION: tuple[int, int] = (1, 0)

Semantic version of the extension API as (major, minor). Third-party packages may inspect this at import time to assert compatibility:

from openviper.ai.extension import EXTENSION_API_VERSION
assert EXTENSION_API_VERSION >= (1, 0), "openviper >= 1.0 required"
class openviper.ai.extension.AIProvider(config)[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for AI model providers.

Implement generate() (and optionally stream()) to integrate a new AI backend with OpenViper.

Example

from openviper.ai.base import AIProvider

class MyProvider(AIProvider):
    name = "myprovider"

    async def generate(self, prompt: str, **kwargs) -> str:
        return "Hello from MyProvider"
Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

name: str = 'base'
abstractmethod async generate(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Generate a text completion for the given prompt.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Provider-specific options (temperature, max_tokens, etc.).

Returns:

Generated text string.

Return type:

str

async stream(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Stream completion tokens.

Default implementation calls generate() and yields the full result as a single chunk. Override for true token-by-token streaming.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Provider-specific options.

Yields:

Incremental text chunks.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

async moderate(content, **kwargs)[source]

Classify content for moderation.

Default implementation sends a structured JSON prompt to generate(). Providers may override for a native moderation API.

Parameters:

  • content (str) – Text to classify.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Forwarded to generate().

Returns:

Dict with keys classification (str), confidence (float 0-1), reason (str), and is_safe (bool).

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

supported_models()[source]

Return the list of model IDs this provider can serve.

Extracts unique model ID values (not display-name keys) from the models and model dicts in the provider config.

Returns:

Sorted list of model ID strings.

Return type:

list[str]

provider_name()[source]

Return the canonical name for this provider (same as the name class attribute).

Return type:

str

async embed(text, **kwargs)[source]

Return an embedding vector for the given text.

Not all providers support embeddings.

Raises:

NotImplementedError – Provider does not support embeddings.

Parameters:

  • text (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

list[float]

async before_inference(prompt, kwargs)[source]

Hook called before each inference. Override to transform input.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – The input prompt.

  • kwargs (dict[str, Any]) – Inference keyword arguments.

Returns:

(modified_prompt, modified_kwargs)

Return type:

tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]

async after_inference(prompt, response)[source]

Hook called after each inference. Override to transform output.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Original prompt.

  • response (str) – Generated response.

Returns:

Modified response.

Return type:

str

async complete(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Alias for generate(). Kept for backward compatibility.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

str

async stream_complete(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Alias for stream(). Kept for backward compatibility.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

class openviper.ai.extension.ProviderRegistry[source]

Bases: object

Thread-safe registry mapping model IDs to provider instances.

Populated automatically from settings.AI_PROVIDERS on first access (double-checked locking ensures this happens exactly once per process).

Third-party providers can be registered without touching core code:

# Programmatic registration
from openviper.ai.registry import provider_registry
provider_registry.register_provider(MyProvider({"model": "my-model"}))

# From a module that exposes get_providers() or PROVIDERS
provider_registry.register_from_module("mypackage.ai.my_provider")

# From a directory of .py provider modules
provider_registry.load_plugins("/etc/myapp/ai_plugins/")

# From installed packages that declare an entry-point
provider_registry.discover_entrypoints()  # group="openviper.ai.providers"
ENTRYPOINT_GROUP = 'openviper.ai.providers'

Entry-point group scanned by discover_entrypoints().

register_provider(provider, *, allow_override=True)[source]

Register all models exposed by provider.

Calls provider.supported_models() and maps every returned model ID to provider.

Parameters:

  • allow_override (bool) – When True (default) an existing mapping for the same model ID is silently replaced with a warning log. Set to False to raise ModelCollisionError instead.

Raises:

ModelCollisionError – allow_override is False and another provider already owns one of the model IDs.

Return type:

None

The module is expected to expose providers through either:

Both conventions are checked; get_providers() takes precedence.

class openviper.ai.providers.openai_provider.OpenAIProvider(config)[source]

Bases: AIProvider

OpenAI GPT provider.

Config:

api_key: Your OpenAI API key. model: Model name (e.g. “gpt-4o”, “gpt-3.5-turbo”). temperature: Sampling temperature. max_tokens: Maximum completion tokens. embed_model: Embedding model name.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

Gemini Provider

Google Gemini AI provider for OpenViper.

Supports Gemini Pro, Ultra, Flash, and Vision models via the google-genai SDK.

Installation:

pip install google-genai

Configuration:
from openviper.ai.registry import ai_registry
from openviper.ai.providers.gemini_provider import GeminiProvider

ai_registry.register("gemini", GeminiProvider, config={
    "api_key": "YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY",
    "model": "gemini-1.5-flash",
})
Environment variable:

GEMINI_API_KEY is read as a fallback when api_key is not in config.

exception openviper.ai.providers.gemini_provider.GeminiError[source]

Bases: Exception

Base exception for Gemini provider errors.

exception openviper.ai.providers.gemini_provider.GeminiAuthError[source]

Bases: GeminiError

Raised when the API key is missing or invalid.

exception openviper.ai.providers.gemini_provider.GeminiRateLimitError[source]

Bases: GeminiError

Raised when the Gemini API rate limit is exceeded.

class openviper.ai.providers.gemini_provider.GeminiProvider(config)[source]

Bases: AIProvider

Google Gemini AI provider.

Config keys:

api_key (str): Google AI API key. Falls back to GEMINI_API_KEY env var. model (str): Default model name. Defaults to "gemini-1.5-flash". temperature (float): Sampling temperature 0–2. Defaults to 1.0. max_output_tokens (int): Maximum tokens in the response. Defaults to 2048. top_p (float | None): Nucleus-sampling probability. top_k (int | None): Top-k sampling. candidate_count (int): Number of candidate responses (default 1). embed_model (str): Embedding model. Defaults to "models/text-embedding-004".

Vision:

Pass images as a list of dicts via **kwargs to complete():

await provider.complete(
    "Describe this image",
    images=[{"mime_type": "image/jpeg", "data": b"<raw bytes>"}],
)

Or pass a URL dict:

images=[{"mime_type": "image/jpeg", "url": "https://example.com/photo.jpg"}]
Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

name = 'gemini'
async generate(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Generate a text completion.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – model (str): Override the default model. images (list): Inline/URL images for vision requests. temperature (float): Sampling temperature. max_output_tokens (int): Max tokens in the response. top_p (float): Nucleus sampling. top_k (int): Top-k sampling. candidate_count (int): Number of candidates.

Returns:

Generated text string.

Raises:

  • GeminiAuthError – Invalid or missing API key.

  • GeminiRateLimitError – Rate limit exceeded.

  • GeminiError – Any other API error.

Return type:

str

async stream(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Stream completion tokens.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Same as generate().

Yields:

Incremental text chunks.

Raises:

  • GeminiAuthError – Invalid or missing API key.

  • GeminiRateLimitError – Rate limit exceeded.

  • GeminiError – Any other API error.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

async embed(text, **kwargs)[source]

Generate an embedding vector for text.

Parameters:

  • text (str) – Input text to embed.

  • **kwargs (Any) –

    model (str): Embedding model. Defaults to

    "models/text-embedding-004".

    task_type (str): One of "RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT",

    "RETRIEVAL_QUERY", "SEMANTIC_SIMILARITY", etc.

Returns:

List of floats (embedding vector).

Raises:

GeminiError – On API failure.

Return type:

list[float]

count_tokens(text)[source]

Estimate the token count for text using a character-based heuristic.

The google-genai SDK exposes client.aio.models.count_tokens() for exact counts, but count_tokens on the base class is synchronous. Callers needing an exact count should call the SDK directly; this provides a fast synchronous estimate (1 token ≈ 4 characters).

Parameters:

text (str) – Text to count tokens in.

Returns:

Estimated token count (minimum 1).

Return type:

int

estimate_cost(input_tokens, output_tokens, model=None)[source]

Estimate API cost for a request.

Parameters:

  • input_tokens (int) – Number of input tokens.

  • output_tokens (int) – Number of output tokens.

  • model (str | None) – Model name (defaults to configured model).

Returns:

Dict with keys "input_cost", "output_cost", "total_cost" (all in USD).

Return type:

dict[str, float]

async embed(text, **kwargs)[source]

Return an embedding vector for the given text.

Not all providers support embeddings.

NotImplementedError – Provider does not support embeddings.

Parameters:

  • text (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

list[float]

Grok Provider

xAI Grok AI provider for OpenViper.

Grok uses an OpenAI-compatible REST API hosted at https://api.x.ai/v1. This provider uses httpx directly — no openai dependency required.

Installation:

pip install openviper[ai] # includes httpx

Configuration:
from openviper.ai.registry import ai_registry
from openviper.ai.providers.grok_provider import GrokProvider

ai_registry.register("grok", GrokProvider, config={
    "api_key": "YOUR_XAI_API_KEY",
    "model": "grok-2-latest",
})
Environment variable:

XAI_API_KEY is read as a fallback when api_key is not in config.

Reasoning (for Grok 3 / grok-2):

Pass reasoning_effort="high" to complete() or stream_complete() to enable chain-of-thought reasoning.

Real-time web search:

Pass search_enabled=True to complete() / stream_complete() to enable Grok’s live internet search capability.

exception openviper.ai.providers.grok_provider.GrokError[source]

Bases: Exception

Base exception for Grok provider errors.

exception openviper.ai.providers.grok_provider.GrokAuthError[source]

Bases: GrokError

Raised when the API key is missing or invalid.

exception openviper.ai.providers.grok_provider.GrokRateLimitError[source]

Bases: GrokError

Raised when the xAI rate limit is exceeded.

class openviper.ai.providers.grok_provider.GrokProvider(config)[source]

Bases: AIProvider

xAI Grok AI provider (OpenAI-compatible REST API via httpx).

Config keys:

  • api_key (str): xAI API key. Falls back to XAI_API_KEY env var.

  • model (str): Default model name. Defaults to "grok-2-latest".

  • temperature (float): Sampling temperature 0–2. Defaults to 1.0.

  • max_tokens (int): Maximum completion tokens. Defaults to 2048.

  • base_url (str): Override the API base URL. Defaults to "https://api.x.ai/v1".

  • timeout (float): HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 60.0.

  • reasoning_effort (str | None): "low", "medium", or "high". Enables chain-of-thought for supported models.

  • search_enabled (bool): Enable real-time web search capability.

Vision:

Pass an images list in **kwargs:

await provider.complete(
    "What's in this image?",
    images=[{"url": "https://example.com/photo.jpg"}],
)

Or supply raw bytes:

import base64
b64 = base64.b64encode(image_bytes).decode()
await provider.complete(
    "Describe the image",
    images=[{"base64": b64, "mime_type": "image/png"}],
)
Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any])

name: str = 'grok'
async generate(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Generate a text completion via the Grok API.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – model (str): Override the default model. temperature (float): Sampling temperature. max_tokens (int): Maximum tokens in the response. images (list): Inline/URL images for vision requests. reasoning_effort (str): "low", "medium", "high". search_enabled (bool): Enable live internet search.

Returns:

Generated text string.

Raises:

  • GrokAuthError – Invalid or missing API key.

  • GrokRateLimitError – Rate limit exceeded.

  • GrokError – Any other API error.

Return type:

str

async stream(prompt, **kwargs)[source]

Stream completion tokens from the Grok API.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – Input text.

  • **kwargs (Any) – Same as generate().

Yields:

Incremental text chunks.

Raises:

  • GrokAuthError – Invalid or missing API key.

  • GrokRateLimitError – Rate limit exceeded.

  • GrokError – Any other API error.

Return type:

AsyncIterator[str]

async embed(text, **kwargs)[source]

Not supported by the Grok API.

Raises:

NotImplementedError – Always.

Parameters:

  • text (str)

  • kwargs (Any)

Return type:

list[float]

count_tokens(text)[source]

Estimate the token count for text.

xAI does not expose a tokeniser endpoint, so this uses a character-based heuristic (1 token ≈ 4 characters).

Parameters:

text (str) – Text to estimate token count for.

Returns:

Estimated token count (minimum 1).

Return type:

int

estimate_cost(input_tokens, output_tokens, model=None)[source]

Estimate API cost for a request.

Parameters:

  • input_tokens (int) – Number of input/prompt tokens.

  • output_tokens (int) – Number of output/completion tokens.

  • model (str | None) – Model name (defaults to configured model).

Returns:

Dict with keys "input_cost", "output_cost", "total_cost" (all in USD).

Return type:

dict[str, float]

openviper.conf

OpenViper settings & configuration system.

Settings are defined as a frozen dataclass, loaded from OPENVIPER_SETTINGS_MODULE and overridable via environment variables or .env files through python-dotenv.

Environment detection uses OPENVIPER_ENV (default: development).

Quick start

Option A — module auto-discovery:

# myproject/settings.py
import dataclasses
from openviper.conf import Settings

@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class MySettings(Settings):
    PROJECT_NAME: str = "MyBlog"
    DATABASE_URL: str = "sqlite:///db.sqlite3"

Then set OPENVIPER_SETTINGS_MODULE=myproject.settings in the environment.

Option B — programmatic configuration:

from openviper.conf import Settings
from openviper.conf.settings import settings

settings.configure(Settings(DATABASE_URL="sqlite:///db.sqlite3"))

configure() must be called before any attribute is first accessed.

class openviper.conf.settings.Settings(PROJECT_NAME='OpenViper Application', VERSION='0.0.1', DEBUG=True, ALLOWED_HOSTS=('localhost', '127.0.0.1'), ROOT_URLCONF='', INSTALLED_APPS=(), USE_TZ=True, TIME_ZONE='UTC', MIDDLEWARE=('openviper.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware', 'openviper.middleware.cors.CORSMiddleware', 'openviper.middleware.auth.AuthenticationMiddleware'), ADMIN_TITLE='OpenViper Admin', ADMIN_HEADER_TITLE='OpenViper', ADMIN_FOOTER_TITLE='OpenViper Admin', SECRET_KEY='INSECURE-CHANGE-ME', DATABASE_URL='', DATABASE_ECHO=False, DATABASE_POOL_SIZE=5, DATABASE_MAX_OVERFLOW=10, DATABASE_POOL_RECYCLE=3600, CACHE_BACKEND='memory', CACHE_URL='', CACHE_TTL=300, PASSWORD_HASHERS=('argon2', 'bcrypt'), SESSION_COOKIE_NAME='sessionid', SESSION_TIMEOUT=<factory>, SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=False, SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True, SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE='Lax', USER_MODEL='openviper.auth.models.User', JWT_ALGORITHM='HS256', JWT_ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE=<factory>, JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE=<factory>, CSRF_COOKIE_NAME='csrftoken', CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE=False, CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY=True, CSRF_COOKIE_SAMESITE='Lax', CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS=(), CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=(), CORS_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS=True, CORS_ALLOWED_METHODS=('GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS'), CORS_ALLOWED_HEADERS=('*', ), CORS_MAX_AGE=600, STATIC_URL='/static/', STATIC_ROOT='./static/', STATICFILES_DIRS=('static/', ), MEDIA_URL='/media/', MEDIA_ROOT='./media/', TEMPLATES_DIR='templates/', TEMPLATE_AUTO_RELOAD=True, LOG_LEVEL='INFO', LOG_FORMAT='text', EMAIL_BACKEND='', EMAIL_HOST='', EMAIL_PORT=587, EMAIL_USE_TLS=True, EMAIL_USER='', EMAIL_PASSWORD='', EMAIL_FROM='', SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=False, SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS=0, SECURE_HSTS_INCLUDE_SUBDOMAINS=False, SECURE_HSTS_PRELOAD=False, SECURE_COOKIES=False, X_FRAME_OPTIONS='DENY', SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER=True, SECURE_CONTENT_SECURITY_POLICY=None, RATE_LIMIT_BACKEND='memory', RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS=1000000, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW=60, RATE_LIMIT_BY='ip', TASKS=<factory>, MODEL_EVENTS=<factory>, ENABLE_AI_PROVIDERS=False, AI_PROVIDERS=<factory>, OPENAPI_TITLE='OpenViper API', OPENAPI_VERSION='0.0.1', OPENAPI_DOCS_URL='/open-api/docs', OPENAPI_REDOC_URL='/open-api/redoc', OPENAPI_SCHEMA_URL='/open-api/openapi.json', OPENAPI_ENABLED=True, ENABLE_MONITORING=False, SENTRY_DSN='', SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT='development', SENTRY_SAMPLE_RATE=0.1, MAX_FILE_SIZE=10485760, STATIC_STORAGE='local', MEDIA_STORAGE='local', AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID='', AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY='', AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME='')[source]

Bases: object

Base configuration dataclass. Subclass and @dataclass(frozen=True) to customise for your project.

All fields are immutable after construction. list defaults from the old class-based system are represented as tuple here.

Parameters:

  • PROJECT_NAME (str)

  • VERSION (str)

  • DEBUG (bool)

  • ALLOWED_HOSTS (tuple[str, ...])

  • ROOT_URLCONF (str)

  • INSTALLED_APPS (tuple[str, ...])

  • USE_TZ (bool)

  • TIME_ZONE (str)

  • MIDDLEWARE (tuple[str, ...])

  • ADMIN_TITLE (str)

  • ADMIN_HEADER_TITLE (str)

  • ADMIN_FOOTER_TITLE (str)

  • SECRET_KEY (str)

  • DATABASE_URL (str)

  • DATABASE_ECHO (bool)

  • DATABASE_POOL_SIZE (int)

  • DATABASE_POOL_RECYCLE (int)

openviper.exceptions

OpenViper exception hierarchy.

All framework exceptions live here. AI-specific exceptions extend AIException which is a subclass of OpenViperException so callers can catch either the broad base or the narrow AI-specific variant.

exception openviper.exceptions.OpenViperException[source]

Bases: Exception

Base exception for all OpenViper errors.

exception openviper.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured[source]

Bases: OpenViperException

Framework or application is incorrectly configured.

exception openviper.exceptions.SettingsValidationError(errors)[source]

Bases: OpenViperException

Settings failed validation on startup.

Parameters:

errors (list[str])

Return type:

None

errors
exception openviper.exceptions.HTTPException(status_code, detail=None, headers=None)[source]

Bases: OpenViperException

Raised to return an HTTP error response.

Parameters:

  • status_code (int) – HTTP status code.

  • detail (Any) – Human-readable error detail.

  • headers (dict[str, str] | None) – Optional extra headers to include in the response.

Return type:

None

status_code
detail
headers

None

openviper.openapi

Auto-generate OpenAPI 3.1.0 schema from route definitions and type hints.

openviper.openapi.schema.request_schema(serializer_cls)[source]

Decorator that attaches a serializer class to a route handler.

The OpenAPI schema generator will use it to produce a requestBody entry so that Swagger UI displays the input form correctly.

Works with both function-based and class-based views.

Example:

from openviper.openapi.schema import request_schema

@router.post("/blogs")
@request_schema(BlogSerializer)
async def create_blog(request):
    ...

For class-based views, prefer setting serializer_class on the View subclass instead — it is picked up automatically.

Parameters:

serializer_cls (type)

Return type:

Any

openviper.openapi.schema.generate_openapi_schema(routes, title='OpenViper API', version='0.0.1', description='')[source]

Build a complete OpenAPI 3.1.0 document from the router’s routes.

Parameters:

  • routes (list[Route]) – All registered Route objects.

  • title (str) – API title.

  • version (str) – API version string.

  • description (str) – API description.

Returns:

OpenAPI 3.1.0 document as a dict.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Swagger UI and ReDoc HTML generators.

openviper.openapi.ui.get_swagger_html(title, openapi_url)[source]

Generate the Swagger UI HTML page.

Parameters:

  • title (str) – API title shown in the browser tab.

  • openapi_url (str) – URL of the OpenAPI JSON schema.

Returns:

Full HTML string for the Swagger UI page.

Return type:

str

openviper.openapi.ui.get_redoc_html(title, openapi_url)[source]

Generate the ReDoc HTML page.

Parameters:

  • title (str) – API title shown in the browser tab.

  • openapi_url (str) – URL of the OpenAPI JSON schema.

Returns:

Full HTML string for the ReDoc page.

Return type:

str

openviper.core

Management Base

Base management command class.

exception openviper.core.management.base.CommandError(message, returncode=1)[source]

Bases: Exception

Raised by management commands to signal a non-zero exit.

Parameters:

  • message (str)

  • returncode (int)

Return type:

None

class openviper.core.management.base.BaseCommand[source]

Bases: object

Abstract base for all OpenViper management commands.

Subclass and implement handle(). Optionally override add_arguments() to register argparse arguments.

Example:

from openviper.core.management.base import BaseCommand

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = "Print hello message"

    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument("name", nargs="?", default="world")

    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        self.stdout(f"Hello, {options['name']}!")
help: str = ''
requires_system_checks: bool = True
stdout(message, ending='\n')[source]
Parameters:

  • message (str)

  • ending (str)

Return type:

None

Parameters:

  • message (str)

  • ending (str)

Return type:

None

message (str)

Return type:

str

message (str)

str

Parameters:

message (str)

str

  • args (Any)

  • options (Any)

Any

Context

Context variables for Openviper.

This module provides global context variables that track request-scoped state such as the currently authenticated user.

openviper.core.context.get_current_user()[source]

Get the currently authenticated user from the context.

Returns None if no user is set (e.g. outside of a request context).

Return type:

Any | None

openviper.core.context.set_current_user(user)[source]

Set the currently authenticated user in the context.

Parameters:

user (Any) – The user object, or AnonymousUser.

Returns:

A token that can be used to restore the previous value.

Return type:

Token

openviper.utils

Data Structures

Utility data structures for headers, query params, and multi-dicts.

Hot-path classes (Headers, QueryParams, ImmutableMultiDict) are backed by multidict C extensions for O(1) lookups and zero-copy iteration.

class openviper.utils.datastructures.Headers(raw)[source]

Bases: object

Immutable, case-insensitive HTTP headers backed by a raw ASGI list.

Lookups are O(1) via a multidict.CIMultiDict C-extension store. The original bytes list is preserved as-is for raw so ASGI callers receive the exact format they expect.

Parameters:

raw (list[list[bytes]] | list[tuple[bytes, bytes]]) – List of [name_bytes, value_bytes] pairs (ASGI format).

  • key (str)

  • default (str | None)

str | None

Return type:

list[str]

keys()[source]
Return type:

list[str]

values()[source]
Return type:

list[str]

items()[source]
Return type:

list[tuple[str, str]]

property raw: list[tuple[bytes, bytes]]
class openviper.utils.datastructures.MutableHeaders(raw=None)[source]

Bases: Headers

Mutable version of Headers for building responses.

Both the bytes list (raw) and the C-backed CIMultiDict are kept in sync so reads stay O(1) even after mutations.

Parameters:

raw (list[list[bytes]] | list[tuple[bytes, bytes]] | None)

Timezone

Timezone utilities for aware and naive datetime handling.

openviper.utils.timezone.get_current_timezone()[source]

Return a ZoneInfo instance for the configured TIME_ZONE.

Return type:

ZoneInfo

openviper.utils.timezone.now()[source]

Return the current time.

If USE_TZ is True, returns an aware UTC datetime. Otherwise, returns a naive local datetime.

Return type:

datetime

openviper.utils.timezone.is_aware(value)[source]

Determine if a given datetime object is aware.

Parameters:

value (datetime)

Return type:

bool

openviper.utils.timezone.is_naive(value)[source]

Determine if a given datetime object is naive.

Parameters:

value (datetime)

Return type:

bool

openviper.utils.timezone.make_aware(value, timezone=None)[source]

Make a naive datetime aware in a specific timezone.

Parameters:

  • value (datetime)

  • timezone (ZoneInfo | None)

Return type:

datetime

openviper.utils.timezone.make_naive(value, timezone=None)[source]

Make an aware datetime naive in a specific timezone.

Parameters:

  • value (datetime)

  • timezone (ZoneInfo | None)

Return type:

datetime

openviper.staticfiles

Static file serving for OpenViper.

class openviper.staticfiles.handlers.NotModifiedResponse(content=None, status_code=200, headers=None, media_type=None)[source]

Bases: Response

Parameters:

  • content (Any)

  • status_code (int)

  • headers (dict[str, str] | None)

  • media_type (str | None)

status_code
media_type
body
class openviper.staticfiles.handlers.StaticFilesMiddleware(app, url_path='/static', directories=None)[source]

Bases: object

ASGI middleware that serves static files from the local filesystem.

Whether this middleware is attached to the app is controlled externally (in app.py) based on settings.DEBUG and whether static() / media() were called in routes.py. Once it is attached it always serves — there is no second DEBUG guard inside the middleware itself.

Usage:

from openviper import OpenViper
from openviper.staticfiles import StaticFilesMiddleware

app = OpenViper()
app = StaticFilesMiddleware(
    app,
    url_path="/static",
    directories=["static", "frontend/dist"],
)
Parameters:

  • app (Any)

  • url_path (str)

  • directories (list[str | Path] | None)

directories: list[Path]
openviper.staticfiles.handlers.collect_static(source_dirs, dest_dir, *, clear=False)[source]

Copy static files from source_dirs and installed apps into dest_dir.

Static content is copied exactly as it exists in each source:

  1. static/ directories discovered inside every installed app (including openviper.admin) are copied first.

  2. Project-level STATICFILES_DIRS are copied next, so they can override app-provided files.

Returns the number of files collected.

Parameters:

  • source_dirs (list[str | Path])

  • dest_dir (str | Path)

  • clear (bool)

Return type:

int

openviper.storage

File storage backends for OpenViper.

Provides a pluggable storage API for handling uploaded files. The default FileSystemStorage persists files to MEDIA_ROOT and serves them at MEDIA_URL.

Usage:

from openviper.storage import default_storage

# Save a file
path = await default_storage.save("uploads/photo.jpg", content)

# Get the public URL
url = default_storage.url(path)

# Delete a file
await default_storage.delete(path)
class openviper.storage.base.Storage[source]

Bases: object

Abstract base for all storage backends.

async save(name, content)[source]

Save a file and return the final storage path (relative).

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Desired file name / path (may be adjusted to avoid collisions).

  • content (bytes | Any) – Raw bytes or a file-like object with a read() method.

Returns:

The relative path where the file was persisted.

Return type:

str

async delete(name)[source]

Delete the file at name. No error if it does not exist.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

None

async exists(name)[source]

Return True if a file already exists at name.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

bool

url(name)[source]

Return the public URL for the file at name.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

str

async size(name)[source]

Return size in bytes of the file at name.

Parameters:

name (str)

Return type:

int

class openviper.storage.base.FileSystemStorage(location=None, base_url=None)[source]

Bases: Storage

Store files on the local filesystem.

Parameters:

  • location (str | None) – Absolute or relative directory path for file storage. Defaults to MEDIA_ROOT from settings.

  • base_url (str | None) – URL prefix for serving files. Defaults to MEDIA_URL from settings.

property location: str
property base_url: str
